| \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/README.ko-KR.md\"\u003e한국어\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA comprehensive suite of Claude Code skills for academic research, covering the full pipeline from research to publication.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInstall in 30 seconds\u003c/strong\u003e (Claude Code CLI / VS Code / JetBrains, v3.7.0+):\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"/plugin marketplace add Imbad0202/academic-research-skills\n/plugin install academic-research-skills\"\u003e\u003cpre lang=\"text\" class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/plugin marketplace add Imbad0202/academic-research-skills\n/plugin install academic-research-skills\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThen try \u003ccode\u003e/ars-plan\u003c/code\u003e to walk through your paper structure via Socratic dialogue, or jump to \u003ca href=\"#quick-install\"\u003eQuick install\u003c/a\u003e for prerequisites and the traditional symlink flow.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAI is your copilot, not the pilot.\u003c/strong\u003e This tool won't write your paper for you. It handles the grunt work — hunting down references, formatting citations, verifying data, checking logical consistency — so you can focus on the parts that actually require your brain: defining the question, choosing the method, interpreting what the data means, and writing the sentence after \"I argue that.\"\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eUnlike a humanizer, this tool doesn't help you hide the fact that you used AI. It helps you write better. Style Calibration learns your voice from past work. Writing Quality Check catches the patterns that make prose feel machine-generated. The goal is quality, not cheating.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhy human-in-the-loop, not full automation?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-why-human-in-the-loop-not-full-automation\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Why human-in-the-loop, not full automation?\" href=\"#why-human-in-the-loop-not-full-automation\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eLu et al. (2026, \u003cem\u003eNature\u003c/em\u003e 651:914-919) built \u003cstrong\u003eThe AI Scientist\u003c/strong\u003e — the first fully autonomous AI research system to publish a paper through blind peer review at a top-tier ML venue (ICLR 2025 workshop, score 6.33/10 vs workshop average 4.87). Their Limitations section enumerates the failure modes that any fully-autonomous AI research pipeline inherits: implementation bugs, hallucinated results, shortcut reliance, bug-as-insight reframing, methodology fabrication, frame-lock, citation hallucinations.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eARS is built on the premise that \u003cstrong\u003ea human researcher augmented by AI avoids these failure modes better than either alone\u003c/strong\u003e. Stage 2.5 and Stage 4.5 integrity gates run a 7-mode blocking checklist (see \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/academic-pipeline/references/ai_research_failure_modes.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline/references/ai_research_failure_modes.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e); the reviewer offers an opt-in calibration mode that measures its own FNR/FPR against a user-supplied gold set.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.07723\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eZhao et al.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/a\u003e (2026-05) audited 111M references across 2.5M papers on arXiv, bioRxiv, SSRN, and PMC. Their conservative estimate is 146,932 hallucinated citations for 2025 alone, with an observed mid-2024 inflection; for the bioRxiv-to-PMC pairing they report 85.3% preprint-to-published persistence. The paper describes \"real citations deployed to support claims the cited references do not actually make\" as an open challenge. ARS v3.7.1 added trust-chain frontmatter for source provenance; v3.7.3 added locator infrastructure (three-layer citation anchors) for future claim-level audits and surfaces advisory risk signals at cite time (ARS labels the claim-faithfulness gap internally as \"L3\"; this is ARS terminology, not the paper's). v3.7.x is motivated by Zhao et al.'s corpus-scale findings; corpus-scale evaluation of ARS itself remains future work.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.8 closes the second half of the L3 gap. v3.7.3 made every citation carry a locator anchor; v3.8 adds an opt-in audit pass (\u003ccode\u003eARS_CLAIM_AUDIT=1\u003c/code\u003e) that fetches the cited source against each anchor and judges whether the claim is actually supported. Five new HIGH-WARN classes (claim-not-supported, negative-constraint-violation, fabricated-reference, anchorless, constraint-violation-uncited) gate-refuse output through the formatter terminal hard gate. Calibration is shipped as a 20-tuple gold set with FNR\u0026lt;0.15 + FPR\u0026lt;0.10 acceptance thresholds; ramp-on plan is deferred to post-calibration evidence per v3.8 spec §5.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.13104\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRen et al.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/a\u003e (2026, \u003cem\u003eSelf-Improvements in Modern Agentic Systems: A Survey\u003c/em\u003e) supplies a third, survey-level anchor. Its scientific-discovery synthesis (§7.4) concludes that discovery agents cannot easily verify novelty, correctness, or reproducibility on their own and may exploit weak proxies instead, must manage evidence across heterogeneous tools and literature, and raise governance issues — \"scientific writing can also amplify misinformation when the evidence is weak.\" Its generation-loop chapters (§5.1–§5.2) list human auditing and retained human anchors among the practical safeguards for self-generated evaluation loops, and its historical chapter (§2.2) records the oldest form of the same lesson: the practical success of Lenat's EURISKO depended heavily on the user serving as the external evaluation signal, pruning unproductive heuristic drift — a limitation the survey notes persists in modern agentic systems. ARS cites the survey as design rationale for its human-in-the-loop stance, not as empirical proof that human-in-the-loop pipelines outperform autonomous ones; the survey's actionable deltas for ARS are tracked in #539–#541 and #547–#550.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.3 was inspired by \u003ca href=\"https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05018\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePaperOrchestra\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/a\u003e (Song, Song, Pfister \u0026amp; Yoon, 2026, Google): Semantic Scholar API verification, anti-leakage protocol, VLM figure verification, and score trajectory tracking.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eArchitecture \u0026amp; pipeline\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-architecture--pipeline\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Architecture \u0026amp; pipeline\" href=\"#architecture--pipeline\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e👉 \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md\"\u003edocs/ARCHITECTURE.md\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — the full pipeline view: flow diagram, stage-by-stage matrix, data-access flow, skill dependency graph, quality gates, and mode list.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe architecture doc supersedes the sprawling pipeline description that used to live here. Everything about \u003cem\u003ewhat runs in which stage\u003c/em\u003e now lives in one place.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eQuick install\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-quick-install\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Quick install\" href=\"#quick-install\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePrerequisites\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/setup\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eClaude Code\u003c/a\u003e (latest; plugin packaging requires recent versions)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eANTHROPIC_API_KEY\u003c/code\u003e exported, or set on first \u003ccode\u003eclaude\u003c/code\u003e run\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cem\u003eOptional:\u003c/em\u003e Pandoc for DOCX, tectonic + Source Han Serif TC for APA 7.0 PDF (Markdown output works without either)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cem\u003eOptional (real Python):\u003c/em\u003e The core skills (research / write / review) need no Python — they are prompt-driven. A \u003cstrong\u003ereal Python interpreter\u003c/strong\u003e is needed only for: the \u003ccode\u003ePreToolUse\u003c/code\u003e write-scope guard (optional subagent hardening — if no real Python is found it cleanly no-ops and the guard is simply inactive; core skills are unaffected), plus a few opt-in features that shell out to Python (revision-patch mode, the submission-package verifier, and the \u003ccode\u003e/ars-cache-invalidate\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003e/ars-mark-read\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003e/ars-unmark-read\u003c/code\u003e commands). On Windows, note that \u003ccode\u003epython3\u003c/code\u003e is often a non-functional Microsoft Store placeholder rather than real Python; install Python from python.org (or via \u003ccode\u003ewinget\u003c/code\u003e) so the launcher can find a real interpreter. The guard launcher is a POSIX shell script and \u003ccode\u003ehooks.json\u003c/code\u003e invokes it through \u003ccode\u003ebash\u003c/code\u003e, so on Windows it needs \u003cstrong\u003eGit Bash\u003c/strong\u003e (bundled with Git for Windows). With Git Bash present, a missing real Python degrades cleanly (the guard no-ops, silently). Without Git Bash, Claude Code falls back to PowerShell, which cannot run the \u003ccode\u003e.sh\u003c/code\u003e launcher at all: the guard is inactive and the \u003ccode\u003ePreToolUse\u003c/code\u003e hook will log an error per call rather than no-op quietly (accepted degradation — the guard is optional and never blocks your writes, but the hook noise is the trade-off until Git Bash is installed).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePlugin install (v3.7.0+, recommended):\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"/plugin marketplace add Imbad0202/academic-research-skills\n/plugin install academic-research-skills\"\u003e\u003cpre lang=\"text\" class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/plugin marketplace add Imbad0202/academic-research-skills\n/plugin install academic-research-skills\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVerify it works:\u003c/strong\u003e run \u003ccode\u003e/ars-plan\u003c/code\u003e and describe a paper you're working on — ARS will start a Socratic dialogue to map out chapter structure. For a single-shot test instead, try \u003ccode\u003e/ars-lit-review \"your topic\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e👉 \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/docs/SETUP.md\"\u003edocs/SETUP.md\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — full guide: install Claude Code, set up API keys, optional Pandoc/tectonic for DOCX/PDF, cross-model verification (\u003ccode\u003eARS_CROSS_MODEL\u003c/code\u003e), and six installation methods (Plugin, project skills, global skills, claude.ai Project, repo-cloned, Claude Science import).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUsing Claude Science?\u003c/strong\u003e The four skills import directly: \u003cstrong\u003eSkills → Import from GitHub\u003c/strong\u003e, paste \u003ccode\u003ehttps://github.com/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills\u003c/code\u003e, \u003cstrong\u003ePreview\u003c/strong\u003e, then \u003cstrong\u003eImport 4 skills\u003c/strong\u003e (requires v3.14.0+ of this repo — the importer reads the explicit skill paths in the marketplace manifest). Imports are point-in-time snapshots: re-import after ARS updates. Imported skills carry the ARS methodology (research / writing / review protocols); Claude Code-specific machinery — slash commands, hooks, subagent orchestration — does not transfer. See \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/docs/SETUP.md\"\u003edocs/SETUP.md\u003c/a\u003e Method 5 for details.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUsing Codex CLI?\u003c/strong\u003e Install the sibling distribution instead: \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills-codex\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eImbad0202/academic-research-skills-codex\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e — same workflow content, Codex-native packaging as a single \u003ccode\u003e$academic-research-suite\u003c/code\u003e skill with \u003ccode\u003ears-*\u003c/code\u003e aliases.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp with Socratic guided mode, PRISMA systematic review, intent detection, dialogue health monitoring, optional cross-model DA, Semantic Scholar API verification.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAcademic Paper\u003c/strong\u003e — 12-agent paper writing with Style Calibration, Writing Quality Check, LaTeX hardening, visualization, revision coaching, citation conversion, anti-leakage protocol, and VLM figure verification.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAcademic Paper Reviewer\u003c/strong\u003e — 7-agent multi-perspective peer review with 0–100 quality rubrics (EIC + 3 dynamic reviewers + Devil's Advocate), concession threshold protocol, attack intensity preservation, optional cross-model DA critique / calibration, R\u0026amp;R traceability matrix, read-only constraint.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAcademic Pipeline\u003c/strong\u003e — 10-stage pipeline orchestrator with adaptive checkpoints, claim verification, Material Passport, optional \u003ccode\u003erepro_lock\u003c/code\u003e, optional cross-model integrity verification, mid-conversation reinforcement, and score trajectory tracking.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eData Access Level Metadata\u003c/strong\u003e (v3.3.2+) — every skill declares \u003ccode\u003edata_access_level\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eraw\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eredacted\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003everified_only\u003c/code\u003e); enforced by \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_data_access_level.py\u003c/code\u003e. Pattern adapted from Anthropic's automated-w2s-researcher (2026). See \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/ground_truth_isolation_pattern.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eshared/ground_truth_isolation_pattern.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTask Type Annotation\u003c/strong\u003e (v3.3.2+) — every skill declares \u003ccode\u003etask_type\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eopen-ended\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003eoutcome-gradable\u003c/code\u003e). All current ARS skills are \u003ccode\u003eopen-ended\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBenchmark Report Schema\u003c/strong\u003e (v3.3.5+) — JSON Schema + lint for honest benchmark comparisons. See \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/benchmark_report_pattern.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eshared/benchmark_report_pattern.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eArtifact Reproducibility Lockfile\u003c/strong\u003e (v3.3.5+) — optional \u003ccode\u003erepro_lock\u003c/code\u003e sub-block on Material Passport. \u003cstrong\u003eConfiguration documentation, not replay guarantee\u003c/strong\u003e — LLM outputs are not byte-reproducible. See \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/artifact_reproducibility_pattern.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eshared/artifact_reproducibility_pattern.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eModel Tiering\u003c/strong\u003e (#517, v3.16+) — optional \u003ccode\u003eARS_MODEL_TIERING\u003c/code\u003e switch with two directions: \u003ccode\u003eeconomy\u003c/code\u003e (execution-type agents dispatch one tier below the session model, floor Opus-class) and \u003ccode\u003equality-boost\u003c/code\u003e (judgment-type agents at integrity gates and final review step up to the frontier tier). Default unset = byte-equivalent to pre-#517 behavior. See \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/model_tiering.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eshared/model_tiering.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCanonical Cross-Model Handoff Envelope\u003c/strong\u003e (#527, v3.17+) — the owner→dispatcher→owner blind-checkpoint transport path (#523) now has a machine-stable \u003ccode\u003e[CROSS-MODEL-HANDOFF v1]\u003c/code\u003e envelope with a normative Python grammar (\u003ccode\u003escripts/cross_model_handoff.py\u003c/code\u003e) instead of prose-only enforcement, pinning agreement/divergence/malformed-result routing across all three checkpoint owners. See \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/cross_model_verification.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eshared/cross_model_verification.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e §\"Cross-model handoff envelope\".\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eExperiment Provenance Intake\u003c/strong\u003e (#260) — optional \u003ccode\u003eexperiment_provenance[]\u003c/code\u003e on the Material Passport records experiments the scholar ran \u003cstrong\u003eexternally\u003c/strong\u003e (ARS never runs experiments), and manuscript claims join to them via \u003ccode\u003eclaim_intent_manifest.planned_experiment_ids[]\u003c/code\u003e. The integrity gate (Stage 2.5/4.5) audits each experiment-backed claim against declared provenance — \u003ccode\u003eALIGNED\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eOVERSTATED\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eNOT_SUPPORTED_BY_PROVENANCE\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ePROVENANCE_INSUFFICIENT\u003c/code\u003e — \u003cstrong\u003ewithout judging whether the experiment itself was correct\u003c/strong\u003e. A fail-closed \u003ccode\u003eexperiment_intake_declaration\u003c/code\u003e makes \"did you run experiments?\" an explicit Stage 1 decision (even literature-only runs declare \u003ccode\u003eno_experiments_declared\u003c/code\u003e). See \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/handoff_schemas.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eshared/handoff_schemas.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e §\"Experiment Provenance Intake (#260)\".\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eShowcase: \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/Imbad0202/experiment-agent\"\u003eExperiment Agent\u003c/a\u003e skill fills the gap between ARS Stage 1 (RESEARCH) and Stage 2 (WRITE).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"ARS Stage 1 RESEARCH  →  RQ Brief + Methodology Blueprint\n        ↓\n  experiment-agent     →  run/manage experiments → validate results\n        ↓\nARS Stage 2 WRITE     →  write paper with verified experiment results\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eARS Stage 1 RESEARCH  →  RQ Brief + Methodology Blueprint\n        ↓\n  experiment-agent     →  run/manage experiments → validate results\n        ↓\nARS Stage 2 WRITE     →  write paper with verified experiment results\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat it does\u003c/strong\u003e: executes code experiments (Python, R, etc.) with real-time monitoring, manages human study protocols with IRB ethics checklist, interprets statistics with 11-type fallacy detection, and verifies reproducibility.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHow to use together\u003c/strong\u003e: pause the ARS pipeline after Stage 1, run experiments in a separate experiment-agent session, then bring the results (with Material Passport) back to ARS Stage 2. ARS requires zero modification. See the \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/Imbad0202/experiment-agent\"\u003eexperiment-agent README\u003c/a\u003e for setup instructions.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStage 1 intake declaration (#260)\u003c/strong\u003e: at Stage 1, ARS detects whether the run will carry experiment-backed claims and sets a fail-closed \u003ccode\u003eexperiment_intake_declaration\u003c/code\u003e on the Material Passport. If you ran experiments externally, the scholar enters one \u003ccode\u003eexperiment_provenance[]\u003c/code\u003e entry per experiment (\u003ccode\u003eexperiment_id\u003c/code\u003e, nested \u003ccode\u003erepro_lock\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eplanned_vs_executed[]\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003enegative_results[]\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eknown_limitations[]\u003c/code\u003e) and the declaration is set to \u003ccode\u003eexperiments_declared\u003c/code\u003e; if not, it is set to \u003ccode\u003eno_experiments_declared\u003c/code\u003e. The declaration is \u003cstrong\u003erequired on every post-#260 passport\u003c/strong\u003e — a run that touches no experiments still declares \u003ccode\u003eno_experiments_declared\u003c/code\u003e, so the integrity gate can never be silently bypassed by a forgotten provenance block. The \u003ccode\u003eexperiment_id\u003c/code\u003es are frozen at this intake point; the writers later reference them via \u003ccode\u003eplanned_experiment_ids[]\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTeaching-side companion\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/YujxZJCN/teaching-skills\"\u003eTeaching Skills\u003c/a\u003e applies the ARS architecture (skill ensembles, shared contracts, staged gates, a Course Passport) to the teaching side of academic life — course design → lessons → assessment → delivery → reflection; its \u003ccode\u003esotl\u003c/code\u003e mode hands classroom-inquiry projects off to ARS deep-research / academic-paper for the publication phase.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" MANDATORY integrity gates (2.5 / 4.5) explicitly skip the observer so compliance checks are not diluted. Based on Wang \u0026amp; Zhang (2026), IJETHE 23:11. dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhat happened\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-what-happened\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What happened\" href=\"#what-happened\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhile using ARS to write a reflection article about AI in higher education, I ran into three structural problems that no amount of prompt engineering could fix:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFrame-lock\u003c/strong\u003e: I asked the AI to run a devil's advocate debate against its own thesis. It did — four rounds, each more refined than the last. But every round stayed inside the frame I'd set. The DA attacked arguments, never premises. It never asked \"are we even discussing the right question?\" This is the same pattern that caused the 31% citation error rate in v2.7's stress test: the verifying AI and the generating AI share the same cognitive frame.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSycophancy under pushback\u003c/strong\u003e: Every time I challenged the DA's attacks, it conceded too quickly. It retracted findings faster than it launched them. The model's training rewards conversational harmony — so \"the user pushed back\" was treated as evidence that the attack was wrong, when often it just meant the user was persistent.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIntent misdetection\u003c/strong\u003e: The Socratic Mentor kept trying to converge and produce deliverables (\"Want me to write this up?\") when I was still exploring. It couldn't distinguish \"the user wants a deep philosophical discussion\" from \"the user wants an RQ brief.\" Both look like engagement, but they need opposite AI behaviors.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhat we changed (v3.0)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-what-we-changed-v30\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What we changed (v3.0)\" href=\"#what-we-changed-v30\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDevil's Advocate — Concession Threshold Protocol\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003edeep-research\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper-reviewer\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDA must now score every rebuttal on a 1-5 scale before responding\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eConcession only allowed at score ≥4 (rebuttal directly addresses core attack with evidence)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eScore ≤3: hold position and restate the original attack\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAnti-sycophancy rules: no consecutive concessions, concession rate tracking, frame-lock detection after each checkpoint\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSocratic Mentor — Intent Detection Layer\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003edeep-research\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eClassifies user intent as exploratory vs. goal-oriented at dialogue start and every 3 turns\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eExploratory mode: disables auto-convergence, raises max rounds to 60, prohibits \"want me to summarize?\" prompts\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eGoal-oriented mode: standard convergence behavior\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAnti-premature-closure rules: in exploratory mode, the user decides when to stop\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSocratic Mentor — Dialogue Health Indicator\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003edeep-research\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSilent self-assessment every 5 turns on three dimensions: persistent agreement, conflict avoidance, premature convergence\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAuto-injects challenging questions when agreement pattern detected\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInvisible to user (to prevent gaming), but log available for post-session review\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhy this matters\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-why-this-matters\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Why this matters\" href=\"#why-this-matters\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThese optimizations don't solve AI's structural limits — they make the limits visible and manageable. The DA will still eventually concede if pushed hard enough. The Socratic Mentor will still have some convergence bias. But now there are explicit checkpoints that slow down the sycophancy, force the DA to justify concessions, and prevent the Mentor from wrapping up before the user is ready.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe deeper lesson: AI literacy isn't about learning to use AI as a tool, following ethics rules, or fearing AI risks. It's about engaging AI deeply enough to discover its structural limits yourself — and your own thinking limits in the process.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAdded:\u003c/strong\u003e three advisory-or-opt-in integrity layers plus a launcher fix. \u003cstrong\u003eRevision-round claim-drift guards (#569/#570):\u003c/strong\u003e a claim-strength ladder (\"no silent move along \u003ccode\u003eis associated with \u0026lt; predicts \u0026lt; causes\u003c/code\u003e without an authorizing roadmap item\") wired into revision drafting and a new advisory Phase E6, plus a deterministic numeric/citation token-conservation checker — together they close the epistemic and token halves of the #390 honest-claim residual (a touched block's interior had no fidelity check). Baseline measured on the current frontier model first (\u003ccode\u003eevals/heldout/revision_claim_drift/\u003c/code\u003e), mechanism shape credited to \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/Yila-AI/sci-ssci-skills\"\u003eYila-AI/sci-ssci-skills\u003c/a\u003e. \u003cstrong\u003ePDF read-integrity preflight (#512):\u003c/strong\u003e a three-signal page-count cross-check so a truncated / mispaginated PDF read cannot mint an apparently-valid \u003ccode\u003epage\u003c/code\u003e anchor. \u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003eread_scope\u003c/code\u003e attestation (#513):\u003c/strong\u003e an optional honest-coverage declaration on the human-read ledger (\u003ccode\u003efull_text\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003esections\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eabstract_only\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003etoc_only\u003c/code\u003e) that makes the finalizer's citation promotion read-scope-aware. \u003cstrong\u003eLauncher watchdog fix (#545):\u003c/strong\u003e removes a pipe-stall that blocked every healthy PreToolUse write-scope-guard call for the full wall-clock bound. Suite → v3.19.0; the three underlying skill versions are unchanged.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.18.0 (2026-07-18) — Self-improvement survey integration: advisory quality layers, risk-stratified claim gate, cross-model reviewer \u0026amp; judge tracks\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3180-2026-07-18--self-improvement-survey-integration-advisory-quality-layers-risk-stratified-claim-gate-cross-model-reviewer--judge-tracks\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.18.0 (2026-07-18) — Self-improvement survey integration: advisory quality layers, risk-stratified claim gate, cross-model reviewer \u0026amp; judge tracks\" href=\"#v3180-2026-07-18--self-improvement-survey-integration-advisory-quality-layers-risk-stratified-claim-gate-cross-model-reviewer--judge-tracks\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAdded:\u003c/strong\u003e eight quality mechanisms motivated by Ren et al. (2026, arXiv:2607.13104, \u003cem\u003eSelf-Improvements in Modern Agentic Systems: A Survey\u003c/em\u003e): per-sub-question scope bindings + a Phase E scope-conformance advisory (#547) and search-bounded novelty claims + an E5 novelty classification (#548) — both advisory-only, displayed per-row at the MANDATORY integrity checkpoints; risk-stratified Stage 2.5 claim verification (100% of HIGH-IMPACT claims + a random sentinel, extending the #518 reference tiers to claim level, #549); cache-through wired into the citation-verification gate with an age-based staleness advisory + opt-in live re-validation (#541, closing the v3.11 Delta-2 forward-decl); a consent-gated cross-model reviewer track — one seat of the fixed five-seat panel on the second model family (#540) — and re-review judge independence with a transparent Judge Record (#539); a metamorphic routing/gate robustness eval seed set (#550), which also shipped the reviewer skill's missing zh-TW trigger aliases; and the survey itself as a third human-in-the-loop literature anchor (#542). Independently of the survey track, plugin installs also gain the #544 SessionStart update-available reminder (\u003ccode\u003e/plugin update\u003c/code\u003e announce when behind; \u003ccode\u003eARS_UPDATE_CHECK=0\u003c/code\u003e kill switch). \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e tracks the suite at v3.18.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.17.0 (2026-07-16) — dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFixed:\u003c/strong\u003e two under-specified pipeline boundaries closed (#528) — Stage 5's \"before finalization: always MANDATORY\" now names exactly one checkpoint (the entry gate between Stage 4.5 PASS and Stage 5 dispatch), and Stage 6 gains a defined terminal-acknowledgement vocabulary (\u003ccode\u003efinish\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003eend\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003edone\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003econfirm\u003c/code\u003e) plus an explicit decline path; all five pipeline surfaces now carry whole-file sha256 content locks (#529) so any further prompt-surface drift fails CI until the hash is updated in the same commit. Blind-checkpoint transport moved to the dispatching layer (#523) — the Bucket A checkpoint owners were being told to execute cross-model transport themselves, which is unexecutable under the runtime Bash deny; the dispatching layer now owns the transport call. \u003cstrong\u003eAdded:\u003c/strong\u003e a canonical \u003ccode\u003e[CROSS-MODEL-HANDOFF v1]\u003c/code\u003e envelope + normative Python grammar (#527) replaces prose-only enforcement of the owner→dispatcher→owner transport path, pinning agreement/divergence/malformed-result routing across all three checkpoint owners. A defrift lock for the #514 tools allowlist (#524, 74 mutation tests) closes the drift path where a symmetric edit to an agent + its mirror could silently re-add Bash. An executable sprint-contract panel checker (#510) recomputes both v3.6.2 decision layers from the primary artifacts and catches a transcription error in the majority-vote formula. A machine-readable degradation registry (#511 Part A) indexes every graceful-degradation mechanism in the suite, plus a hermetic transport-fixture integration test for the citation-verification gate (#511 Part B) exercising all four resolver clients end-to-end against checked-in synthetic API bodies. \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e tracks the suite at v3.17.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.16.0 (2026-07-12) — Model tiering, cross-model gate hardening, WP advisory sharpening\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3160-2026-07-12--model-tiering-cross-model-gate-hardening-wp-advisory-sharpening\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.16.0 (2026-07-12) — Model tiering, cross-model gate hardening, WP advisory sharpening\" href=\"#v3160-2026-07-12--model-tiering-cross-model-gate-hardening-wp-advisory-sharpening\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAdded:\u003c/strong\u003e opt-in model tiering (#517) — a new \u003ccode\u003eARS_MODEL_TIERING\u003c/code\u003e switch with two directions (\u003ccode\u003eeconomy\u003c/code\u003e dispatches the 13 execution-type agents one tier below the session model, floor Opus-class; \u003ccode\u003equality-boost\u003c/code\u003e steps the judgment-type agents at the integrity gates and final-review surfaces up to the frontier tier); default unset stays byte-equivalent, with the frozen 39-agent classification pinned by a new manifest + lint. Cross-model gate hardening (#518) — risk-stratified verification sampling (HIGH-IMPACT references verified 100% at both gates), blind disagreement checkpoints at the two irreversible decisions (design freeze + final editorial decision), an id-status allowlist for verifier model ids, and a promotion bakeoff protocol; the once-planned generic 6th reviewer is retired, not deferred. GPT-5.6 Sol listed as a provisional cross-model verifier with explicit reasoning-effort control (#515). Korean trigger keywords + routing boundary fixtures by devCharlotte (#452/#509). A CARS introduction-rhetoric + title-crafting reference for the paper writer (#500). \u003cstrong\u003eChanged:\u003c/strong\u003e the WP research-question advisory generalizes beyond its 20-shell table via the noun-swap test (#501) and a sharpened exemption clause that catches decorated title-form shells (#505) — held-out miss rate 0.34–0.38 → 0.094 with false-fire 0/16 preserved; reviewer calibration protocol now documents the LLM-as-judge leniency direction (FARS anchor, #484); OpenAlex API-key auth + budget-aware 429 handling + arXiv ToU-aligned backoff (#495/#496). \u003cstrong\u003eDocs:\u003c/strong\u003e THIRD_PARTY.md community directory (#497/#498). \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e tracks the suite at v3.16.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.15.0 (2026-07-04) — Release-gate hardening, prompt-debt retirement round 2, defrift locks\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3150-2026-07-04--release-gate-hardening-prompt-debt-retirement-round-2-defrift-locks\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.15.0 (2026-07-04) — Release-gate hardening, prompt-debt retirement round 2, defrift locks\" href=\"#v3150-2026-07-04--release-gate-hardening-prompt-debt-retirement-round-2-defrift-locks\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA release-discipline-and-hygiene release; no skill-behavior changes. \u003cstrong\u003eAdded:\u003c/strong\u003e three CI gates — the CHANGELOG-covers-merges pre-tag gate (#483), version-consistency invariants 9-11 plus a tag-time re-run gate (#487), and a command-invariants gate pinning the SessionStart announce list to the actual 16-command inventory (#486) — plus two defrift locks: the Phase Boundary enforcement sentence is pinned verbatim across all 23 Bucket A agent blocks, and the SETUP cross-model examples are pinned to each other and to the canonical model tables (#491 → #492). \u003cstrong\u003eChanged:\u003c/strong\u003e prompt-debt retirement round 2 deep-scans the 17 agents the first pass deferred (#489 → #490): two live self-contradictions fixed in both socratic_mentor agents (stale 15-round quit rules vs the documented typical 20-30-round run), the repo-wide stale enforcement-status sentence corrected at 29 surfaces, few-shot and duplicated-process scaffolds trimmed across 7 agents — verified by a 4-batch parallel audit + independent codex cross-model challenge; audit report under \u003ccode\u003eaudits/\u003c/code\u003e. \u003cstrong\u003eFixed:\u003c/strong\u003e DOI badge served from shields.io (#482). \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e tracks the suite at v3.15.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.14.0 (2026-07-02) — Claude Science importability, eval-comment rendering, prompt-debt retirement\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3140-2026-07-02--claude-science-importability-eval-comment-rendering-prompt-debt-retirement\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.14.0 (2026-07-02) — Claude Science importability, eval-comment rendering, prompt-debt retirement\" href=\"#v3140-2026-07-02--claude-science-importability-eval-comment-rendering-prompt-debt-retirement\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA portability-and-polish release; no skill-behavior changes. \u003cstrong\u003eAdded:\u003c/strong\u003e Claude Science importability — the marketplace manifest declares explicit skill paths, so GitHub-API importers that cannot traverse the symlinked \u003ccode\u003eskills/\u003c/code\u003e directory (Claude Science \"Import from GitHub\", Windows checkouts) now find all four skills; verified end-to-end on Claude Science, with an import guide in README + SETUP (#480). Eval-harness PR comments render as a one-line verdict + per-task table with the raw JSON folded into \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;details\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e, replacing the raw report dump — display layer only, gate logic byte-identical (#479). \u003cstrong\u003eChanged:\u003c/strong\u003e expired writing-harness scaffolds retired from four writer-surface agents after the 2026-07 harness-retirement audit (#476/#477 → #478, net −111 prompt lines); a remind-don't-block Platform Port Reminder surfaces the platform-ports policy when a PR adds a new top-level directory (#473). \u003cstrong\u003eDocs:\u003c/strong\u003e native-reviewed Korean README by devCharlotte (#469/#471); GitHub Copilot repository instructions (#465); auto permission mode recommended over Skip Permissions (#464). The accumulated \u003ccode\u003e[Unreleased]\u003c/code\u003e backlog (16 entries whose code shipped before the v3.13.0 tag — diff/patch revision mode #390, submission-package verifier #394, eval gold sets #215/#216, and more) is rolled into the versioned record; see \u003ccode\u003eCHANGELOG.md\u003c/code\u003e. \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e tracks the suite at v3.14.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" minor release hardening the install/runtime surface and extending cross-model reach. \u003cstrong\u003eFixes:\u003c/strong\u003e the write-scope guard no longer false-denies a user's own \u003ccode\u003eCLAUDE.md\u003c/code\u003e under the git-clone + symlink install layout (#459, closing the residual half of #448/#449 — \u003ccode\u003eCLAUDE.md\u003c/code\u003e is documentation, not a load-bearing enforcement file, so it leaves the infra-protected list while every load-bearing file stays protected); Windows Python hook portability + graceful no-Python degradation via a cross-platform \u003ccode\u003ehooks/run_guard.sh\u003c/code\u003e launcher that rejects the 0-byte Microsoft Store \u003ccode\u003epython3\u003c/code\u003e stub and never spams the hook log (#454); \u003ccode\u003edraft_writer\u003c/code\u003e dual-phase static union documented + POSIX-safe Windows path matching (#451). \u003cstrong\u003eAdded:\u003c/strong\u003e provider-agnostic cross-model verification accepting OpenAI-compatible endpoints (MiMo, DeepSeek, self-hosted) alongside grounded first-party OpenAI, which is never silently downgraded (#455); an opt-in Socratic adjacent-framing probe (STORM-borrowed perspective expansion, \u003ccode\u003eARS_SOCRATIC_ADJACENT_PROBE=1\u003c/code\u003e, default OFF, prose-layer only — \u003ccode\u003edeep-research\u003c/code\u003e 2.10.0 → 2.11.0) (#461). \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e tracks the suite at v3.13.0; \u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper-reviewer\u003c/code\u003e are unchanged. See \u003ccode\u003eCHANGELOG.md\u003c/code\u003e for the per-issue detail.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.12.1 (2026-06-15) — Reviewer-response triage modes (PR #433 integration)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3121-2026-06-15--reviewer-response-triage-modes-pr-433-integration\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.12.1 (2026-06-15) — Reviewer-response triage modes (PR #433 integration)\" href=\"#v3121-2026-06-15--reviewer-response-triage-modes-pr-433-integration\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA patch release folding the genuinely-novel parts of an external contribution into existing skills as modes, per ARS's mode-based architecture. \u003cstrong\u003eNew modes:\u003c/strong\u003e \u003ccode\u003edeep-research\u003c/code\u003e \u003ccode\u003ethree-way-scan\u003c/code\u003e — a lightweight WHY/HOW/WHAT paper-comparison triage between \u003ccode\u003equick\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003elit-review\u003c/code\u003e, with per-paper shortlists + a cross-paper synthesis (\u003ccode\u003edeep-research\u003c/code\u003e 2.9.4 → 2.10.0); \u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper\u003c/code\u003e \u003ccode\u003erebuttal-audit\u003c/code\u003e — standalone advisory QA of an author's existing rebuttal/response draft against the reviewer comments (per-comment coverage table + gap list + tone/evidence/misread risk flags), which generates nothing and explicitly suppresses Schema 11 / Material Passport writes / \u003ccode\u003eready_to_submit\u003c/code\u003e when run standalone (enforced by a \u003ccode\u003echeck_rebuttal_audit_guard()\u003c/code\u003e lint with mutation coverage); plus a \u003ccode\u003erevision-coach\u003c/code\u003e scope extension to pushback/disagreement posture and non-journal scopes, and \u003ccode\u003e/ars-3w\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003e/ars-rebuttal-audit\u003c/code\u003e slash commands. Routed by input shape: reviewer comments AND a draft → \u003ccode\u003erebuttal-audit\u003c/code\u003e; comments only → \u003ccode\u003erevision-coach\u003c/code\u003e. Integrated from \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/Yaobin29\"\u003e@Yaobin29\u003c/a\u003e's \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/pull/433\" data-hovercard-type=\"pull_request\" data-hovercard-url=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/pull/433/hovercard\"\u003ePR #433\u003c/a\u003e. Suite mode count 25 → 27 (still 4 skills). See \u003ccode\u003eCHANGELOG.md\u003c/code\u003e for the per-issue detail.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.12.0 (2026-06-08) — Kong auto-research feature track: experiment provenance, figure fidelity, cross-paper contradiction, partial-evidence decomposition\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3120-2026-06-08--kong-auto-research-feature-track-experiment-provenance-figure-fidelity-cross-paper-contradiction-partial-evidence-decomposition\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.12.0 (2026-06-08) — Kong auto-research feature track: experiment provenance, figure fidelity, cross-paper contradiction, partial-evidence decomposition\" href=\"#v3120-2026-06-08--kong-auto-research-feature-track-experiment-provenance-figure-fidelity-cross-paper-contradiction-partial-evidence-decomposition\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA minor release shipping the Kong et al. (2026, arXiv:2605.18661) auto-research feature track plus the partial-evidence-trap decomposition work, each reviewed and merged independently. \u003cstrong\u003eNew features:\u003c/strong\u003e Experiment Provenance Intake + claim→experiment alignment — a schema-first evidence-ledger layer for experiment-backed claims, intake-and-alignment only (the scholar runs experiments externally; ARS never executes them) (#260); a Figure/Table Fidelity Gate that checks whether a caption's interpretation follows from the data and whether the manuscript cites the artifact for a claim it supports (#261); a structured Cross-Paper Contradiction inventory making assessed paper-pairs enumerable for scholar confirmation (#262); and sub-claim decomposition before judgment in both the citation judge (#213) and the editorial synthesizer (#214), closing the §F.3.2 partial-evidence trap on both layers. \u003cstrong\u003eGuidance + interpretive layer:\u003c/strong\u003e concise-output + pressure-stable boundary reinforcement across the report-producing reviewers (#274); a same-family / rubric-aware calibration epistemic note (#273); the retrieved-content instruction/data boundary stated as a standing principle (#367). \u003cstrong\u003eNegative scope:\u003c/strong\u003e the Kong META (#255) closed with a \"Rejected mechanisms\" section in \u003ccode\u003ePOSITIONING.md\u003c/code\u003e enumerating the five autonomous mechanisms ARS does not do, plus two Tier D design-lesson docs. \u003cstrong\u003eRelease-discipline lint:\u003c/strong\u003e version-consistency invariants 5–7 (#357) and ARCHITECTURE component-version policing (#345). Plus correctness fixes across the cross-model grounding guards (#346 / #349 / #351), the citation-gate cache key and rationale bounding (#359 / #360 / #361), the eval gold set (#250), and ACL/EMNLP disclosure regrounding (#242). The new schemas, manifest field, and all invariants are additive and backward-compatible. \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e tracks the suite at v3.12.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged. See \u003ccode\u003eCHANGELOG.md\u003c/code\u003e for the per-issue detail.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.11.1 (2026-06-06) — Post-ship correctness, hardening \u0026amp; provenance rollup\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3111-2026-06-06--post-ship-correctness-hardening--provenance-rollup\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.11.1 (2026-06-06) — Post-ship correctness, hardening \u0026amp; provenance rollup\" href=\"#v3111-2026-06-06--post-ship-correctness-hardening--provenance-rollup\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA patch release rolling up the post-ship fixes surfaced after v3.11.0, each reviewed and merged independently: a cross-model consent-gate extension to the integrity-verification + collaboration-depth paths (#322), a per-entry OpenAlex + Crossref backfill parallelization (#138), and seven correctness/hardening fixes across the citation-existence gate, the v3.10 policy layer, the eval harness, the domain evidence profiles, and the #310 security-boundary edge cases (#323 / #327 / #328 / #329 / #331 / #332 / #333) — including two P1 fixes (#327 domain-profile activation on the no-handoff path, #328 the eval-harness per-class threshold gate). No new features and no breaking schema changes. See \u003ccode\u003eCHANGELOG.md\u003c/code\u003e for the per-issue detail.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.11.0 (2026-06-04) — Deterministic citation verification gate (#182)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3110-2026-06-04--deterministic-citation-verification-gate-182\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.11.0 (2026-06-04) — Deterministic citation verification gate (#182)\" href=\"#v3110-2026-06-04--deterministic-citation-verification-gate-182\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAdds a \u003cstrong\u003edeterministic citation-existence verification gate\u003c/strong\u003e that runs independently of LLM peer review. Every cited reference is cross-checked against up to four bibliographic indexes — Semantic Scholar + OpenAlex + Crossref + the new \u003cstrong\u003earXiv resolver\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003escripts/arxiv_client.py\u003c/code\u003e, no API key needed) — and a per-citation \u003ccode\u003elookup_verified\u003c/code\u003e status (\u003ccode\u003e{true, false, unresolvable}\u003c/code\u003e) is written to a unified summary, so a fabricated citation with a provably-bogus DOI/arXiv ID is caught by lookup rather than by hoping a reviewer agent notices. The gate \u003cstrong\u003einherits the v3.10 \u003ccode\u003eterminal_policies\u003c/code\u003e opt-in model\u003c/strong\u003e: detection always runs, but a \u003ccode\u003elookup_verified == false\u003c/code\u003e row is terminal \u003cstrong\u003eonly\u003c/strong\u003e when a user opts into \u003ccode\u003eterminal_policies.citation_existence == strict\u003c/code\u003e — default behavior is advisory and \u003ccode\u003e/ars-mark-read\u003c/code\u003e-acknowledgeable. \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e is narrowed to \u003cstrong\u003eID-keyed unmatched\u003c/strong\u003e (an exact DOI/arXiv lookup that provably fails), so legitimately-unindexed humanities / non-English / regional citations stay \u003ccode\u003eunresolvable\u003c/code\u003e and never block (a documented precision-over-recall tradeoff). Ships a persistent SQLite verification cache (\u003ccode\u003e~/.cache/ars/verification.db\u003c/code\u003e, 90-day TTL) with an \u003ccode\u003e/ars-cache-invalidate\u003c/code\u003e command, a standalone \u003ccode\u003everification_gate\u003c/code\u003e API + \u003ccode\u003everify_passport.py\u003c/code\u003e CLI, and a four-index extension (k=0..4) of the v3.9.0 contamination triangulation matrix (all advisory). \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e tracks the suite at v3.11.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged. Spec: \u003ccode\u003edocs/design/2026-05-21-v3.10-182-promote-citation-gate-spec.md\u003c/code\u003e (§0 amendment + C-V6).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.10.0 (2026-06-01) — Triangulation policy layer, Kong survey adoptions, eval harness, scoped-write guard\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3100-2026-06-01--triangulation-policy-layer-kong-survey-adoptions-eval-harness-scoped-write-guard\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.10.0 (2026-06-01) — Triangulation policy layer, Kong survey adoptions, eval harness, scoped-write guard\" href=\"#v3100-2026-06-01--triangulation-policy-layer-kong-survey-adoptions-eval-harness-scoped-write-guard\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMinor release bundling: the opt-in contamination-triangulation \u003cstrong\u003eterminal policy layer\u003c/strong\u003e (#127 — default citation behavior byte-equivalent to v3.9.0); \u003cstrong\u003eKong et al. 2026 survey adoptions\u003c/strong\u003e — the Rebuttal Commitment Ledger (#256/#266/#268/#269) and discipline-relative domain evidence profiles (#259); the \u003cstrong\u003ev3.10 measurement infrastructure\u003c/strong\u003e — a generalized eval gold set + ranking-lift CI gate (#184); the \u003cstrong\u003escoped-write guard MVP\u003c/strong\u003e (#134) — a deterministic \u003ccode\u003ePreToolUse\u003c/code\u003e hook that fences the 23 single-phase agents to their own phase directory and denies them Bash (they use the Grep/Glob and structured editing tools instead); the \u003ccode\u003e/ars-mark-read\u003c/code\u003e plugin commands (#190) plus a broken-on-arrival fix (#195); a Simplified-Chinese README (#185); and CI hardening (#156/#155). \u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper\u003c/code\u003e → v3.2.0 and \u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper-reviewer\u003c/code\u003e → v1.10.0 for the Commitment-Ledger and domain-profile additions; \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e tracks the suite at v3.10.0. Default skill behavior is unchanged unless a strict policy mode is opted into; the one default-on change is the #134 guard, which constrains the fenced subagents, not user-facing outputs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.9.4.2 (2026-05-19) — post-ship hotfix for PR #149 CI discipline gates (codex post-ship)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3942-2026-05-19--post-ship-hotfix-for-pr-149-ci-discipline-gates-codex-post-ship\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.9.4.2 (2026-05-19) — post-ship hotfix for PR #149 CI discipline gates (codex post-ship)\" href=\"#v3942-2026-05-19--post-ship-hotfix-for-pr-149-ci-discipline-gates-codex-post-ship\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCodex post-ship review of PR #149 (7 CI discipline gates) surfaced 4 P2 findings; v3.9.4.2 hardens 3 of 4. F1: \u003ccode\u003eharness-retirement-monthly.yml\u003c/code\u003e adds \u003ccode\u003eGH_REPO\u003c/code\u003e so scheduled runs have repo context for \u003ccode\u003egh issue create\u003c/code\u003e. F2: \u003ccode\u003erelease-cooldown.yml\u003c/code\u003e filters \u003ccode\u003ePREV_TAG\u003c/code\u003e lookup to \u003ccode\u003ev*\u003c/code\u003e tags so non-release tags cannot bypass cooldown. F3: \u003ccode\u003erelease-cooldown.yml\u003c/code\u003e also reads annotated tag subject + accepts \u003ccode\u003ehot-fix\u003c/code\u003e spelling (v3.9.2 was previously a false-negative hotfix). PR #157 follow-up: \u003ccode\u003e[skip-cooldown]\u003c/code\u003e override now read from both commit message AND annotated tag message (self-bootstrapping fix — this tag's cooldown bypass demonstrates F2+F3 work end-to-end). F4 (test-count-monotonic harden) reverted because it surfaced pre-existing \u003ccode\u003escripts/\u003c/code\u003e package issue, tracked as #154 (since fixed by PR #158) + re-attempt #155. Closes #152. Follow-ups: #155, #156.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.9.4.1 (2026-05-19) — post-ship hotfix for v3.9.4 temporal verification (#135 codex post-ship)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v3941-2026-05-19--post-ship-hotfix-for-v394-temporal-verification-135-codex-post-ship\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.9.4.1 (2026-05-19) — post-ship hotfix for v3.9.4 temporal verification (#135 codex post-ship)\" href=\"#v3941-2026-05-19--post-ship-hotfix-for-v394-temporal-verification-135-codex-post-ship\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCodex post-ship review of v3.9.4 caught 4 real bugs that per-task subagent reviewers missed. Hotfix patches all 4: (1) \u003ccode\u003eaudit()\u003c/code\u003e now wires \u003ccode\u003ecitation_provenance\u003c/code\u003e through to P2 and P4 — when a ref slug has \u003ccode\u003econfidence: low\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003econflict\u003c/code\u003e, the verifier emits \u003ccode\u003eTEMPORAL-METADATA-MISSING\u003c/code\u003e instead of using timeline dates as ground truth (spec §3.4 first-party safety check was broken). (2) \u003ccode\u003e_date_to_interval\u003c/code\u003e parses all schema-valid date shapes including \u003ccode\u003eYYYY-MM\u003c/code\u003e (Crossref month precision) and \u003ccode\u003eYYYY-MM-DD..YYYY-MM-DD\u003c/code\u003e (interval); v3.9.4 silently \u003ccode\u003eValueError\u003c/code\u003e'd on these and skipped the check. (3) P4 now binds direct date captures when ref markers are absent — sentences like \"The 2026 policy enabled the 2020 rollout\" actually trigger now. (4) \u003ccode\u003ecitation_provenance.schema.json\u003c/code\u003e \u003ccode\u003econfidence:high\u003c/code\u003e allOf now requires presence (\u003ccode\u003ethen.required\u003c/code\u003e) in addition to non-null, closing the absent-property bypass. 1561 passed (+12 new tests vs v3.9.4 baseline, 0 regression). ARCHITECTURE.md aligned to current state (was stale at v3.8.0).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.9.4 (2026-05-18) — #135 temporal verification layer (advisory)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v394-2026-05-18--135-temporal-verification-layer-advisory\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.9.4 (2026-05-18) — #135 temporal verification layer (advisory)\" href=\"#v394-2026-05-18--135-temporal-verification-layer-advisory\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDeterministic advisory verifier at the Phase 4 → 5 boundary covering 5 temporal failure modes (P1 retrospective arithmetic, P2 anachronistic citation, P3 comparator unmaterialized, P4 causal inversion, P5 deictic present).
