Two new optional booleans (\u003ccode\u003eopenalex_unmatched\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ecrossref_unmatched\u003c/code\u003e) on \u003ccode\u003econtamination_signals\u003c/code\u003e; manual-entry not-rule extended symmetrically. Finalizer adds a 4-tier advisory matrix (k=0/1/2/3 over present \u003ccode\u003e*_unmatched\u003c/code\u003e fields) with v3.7.3 legacy \u003ccode\u003eCONTAMINATED-UNMATCHED\u003c/code\u003e preserved for the k=1/k_max=1 S2-only case. Formatter pass-through allowlist extends 3 → 9 suffixes; refusal rules 1-10 unchanged per R-L3-2-E. The policy layer (strict modes, hard-block tier, \u003ccode\u003evenue_type\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003etriangulation_policy\u003c/code\u003e) is deferred to v3.10 per spec §2.3. k=3 marker is \u003ccode\u003eCONTAMINATED-TRIANGULATION-UNMATCHED\u003c/code\u003e (describes observable, not inferred cause). 3 new firm rules: R-L3-2-C (k computed over present fields), R-L3-2-D (no API-inferred classification), R-L3-2-E (refusal list unchanged; pass-through allowlist extends).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eMigration:\u003c/strong\u003e v3.7.3 corpora — run \u003ccode\u003epython scripts/migrate_literature_corpus_to_v3_9_0.py PATH\u003c/code\u003e to backfill the two new fields. Pre-v3.7.3 corpora — run \u003ccode\u003emigrate_literature_corpus_to_v3_7_3.py\u003c/code\u003e FIRST, then v3.9.0 migration (daisy-chained per spec §3.7; the v3.9.0 tool only acts on entries that already carry \u003ccode\u003econtamination_signals.semantic_scholar_unmatched\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.8.2 (2026-05-17) — #118 uncited audit_tool_failure surface\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v382-2026-05-17--118-uncited-audit_tool_failure-surface\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.8.2 (2026-05-17) — #118 uncited audit_tool_failure surface\" href=\"#v382-2026-05-17--118-uncited-audit_tool_failure-surface\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e#118 closure. The \u003ccode\u003eARS_CLAIM_AUDIT=1\u003c/code\u003e uncited constraint-judging path used to silently substitute \u003ccode\u003e{\"judgment\": \"NOT_VIOLATED\"}\u003c/code\u003e on \u003ccode\u003eJudgeInvocationError\u003c/code\u003e, suppressing HIGH-WARN constraint checks on transient judge outage. v3.8.2 routes those failures through a dedicated \u003ccode\u003euncited_audit_failures[]\u003c/code\u003e aggregate at MED-WARN advisory tier, mirroring the cited path INV-14 row but using a dedicated schema because \u003ccode\u003eclaim_audit_result.ref_slug\u003c/code\u003e is required and the uncited path has no ref to bind. The four option-1..4 trade-offs from the #118 issue body landed on option 2 (new aggregate) — option 4 (re-raise and abort) was rejected for the audit-coverage hit on flaky judge endpoints.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNew \u003ccode\u003euncited_audit_failure.schema.json\u003c/code\u003e aggregate\u003c/strong\u003e (spec §3.6). One entry per uncited sentence × manifest pair where the constraint judge raised \u003ccode\u003eJudgeInvocationError\u003c/code\u003e. Same fault-class enum as cited-path INV-14 (\u003ccode\u003ejudge_timeout\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ejudge_api_error\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ejudge_parse_error\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ecache_corruption\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eretrieval_api_error\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eretrieval_timeout\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eretrieval_network_error\u003c/code\u003e). \u003ccode\u003erule_version: D4-c-v1-uaf-v1\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUAF-INV-1..UAF-INV-6 lint\u003c/strong\u003e (spec §6 rule 4d). \u003ccode\u003efinding_id\u003c/code\u003e uniqueness, scoped_manifest_id cross-array integrity, (M, C) pair integrity when manifest_claim_id non-null, per-(sentence, manifest) dedup, rationale fault_class prefix, cross-aggregate exclusivity vs \u003ccode\u003econstraint_violations[]\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFinalizer §5 MED-WARN advisory row\u003c/strong\u003e: annotation \u003ccode\u003e[CLAIM-AUDIT-TOOL-FAILURE-UNCITED — \u0026lt;fault-class\u0026gt;]\u003c/code\u003e, gate passes (retry-next-pass remediation). Formatter REFUSE list unchanged — UAF is advisory.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePipeline integration\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003escripts/claim_audit_pipeline.py\u003c/code\u003e): swallow site at line 1211-1224 removed; \u003ccode\u003eJudgeInvocationError\u003c/code\u003e now emits a UAF row + \u003ccode\u003econtinue\u003c/code\u003es to the next (sentence, manifest) pair. No fake NOT_VIOLATED reaches \u003ccode\u003econstraint_violations[]\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTests\u003c/strong\u003e: 18 new (15 schema/lint TSUAFUncitedAuditFailureInvariants + 3 pipeline integration TP23UncitedJudgeOutageEmitsUAF). Baseline 694 → 712 tests, 0 regression.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAgent doc\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline/agents/claim_ref_alignment_audit_agent.md\u003c/code\u003e): Output emission table grows seventh row; Error handling table grows from 3 surfaces to 4 surfaces with the uncited-path UAF row.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.8.0 (2026-05-16) — L3 Claim-Faithfulness Locator + Audit (paired milestone)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v380-2026-05-16--l3-claim-faithfulness-locator--audit-paired-milestone\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.8.0 (2026-05-16) — L3 Claim-Faithfulness Locator + Audit (paired milestone)\" href=\"#v380-2026-05-16--l3-claim-faithfulness-locator--audit-paired-milestone\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.7.3 + v3.8 close the L3 (claim-faithfulness) gap end-to-end. v3.7.3 ships the locator infrastructure — every citation carries a three-layer anchor so future audits can fetch the cited passage. v3.8 ships the audit pass that consumes those anchors, judges whether the cited source supports the claim, and gate-refuses HIGH-WARN violations at the formatter terminal hard gate. The release also bundles 5 audit-trail-shipped feature PRs accumulated since v3.7.0 (#104 / #105 / #108 / #111 / #115).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e#103 — \u003ccode\u003eclaim_ref_alignment_audit_agent\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e (v3.8 PR #121). Opt-in (\u003ccode\u003eARS_CLAIM_AUDIT=1\u003c/code\u003e, default OFF) Stage 4→5 audit agent. Judges every sampled citation against retrieved excerpt; emits \u003ccode\u003eclaim_audit_results[]\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003eclaim_intent_manifests[]\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003eclaim_drifts[]\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003euncited_assertions[]\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003econstraint_violations[]\u003c/code\u003e aggregates. 8-row finalizer matrix routes HIGH-WARN classes (CLAIM-NOT-SUPPORTED / NEGATIVE-CONSTRAINT-VIOLATION / FABRICATED-REFERENCE / ANCHORLESS / CONSTRAINT-VIOLATION-UNCITED) through the formatter REFUSE rules 6-10. Calibration runner ships with 20-tuple gold set (T-C1 FNR\u0026lt;0.15 + FPR\u0026lt;0.10, T-C2 per-class, T-C3 shape integrity). 8 rounds of dual-track review (R1 codex + Gemini-3.1-pro-preview, R2-R8 codex-only after Gemini quota exhausted); trajectory R1 4P1+2P2 → R8 0P1+4P2 ship gate.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ev3.7.3 — Three-Layer Citation Emission + contamination signals\u003c/strong\u003e (PR #98). \u003ccode\u003esynthesis_agent\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003edraft_writer_agent\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ereport_compiler_agent\u003c/code\u003e gain \u003ccode\u003e## Three-Layer Citation Emission (v3.7.3)\u003c/code\u003e H2. Every \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;!--ref:slug--\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e carries \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;!--anchor:\u0026lt;kind\u0026gt;:\u0026lt;value\u0026gt;--\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e with \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;kind\u0026gt; ∈ {quote, page, section, paragraph, none}\u003c/code\u003e (quote anchors capped at 25 words, URL-encoded). \u003ccode\u003epipeline_orchestrator_agent\u003c/code\u003e finalizer becomes 5-cell with precedence-zero NO-LOCATOR check. \u003ccode\u003eformatter_agent\u003c/code\u003e adds explicit hard-gate refusal for \u003ccode\u003e[UNVERIFIED CITATION — NO QUOTE OR PAGE LOCATOR]\u003c/code\u003e. \u003ccode\u003eliterature_corpus_entry.schema.json\u003c/code\u003e adds optional \u003ccode\u003econtamination_signals: { preprint_post_llm_inflection, semantic_scholar_unmatched }\u003c/code\u003e object. \u003ccode\u003ebibliography_agent\u003c/code\u003e computes both signals at ingest. 11-round review trajectory (Codex×10 + Gemini cross-model×1) closed 22 findings. Spec: \u003ccode\u003edocs/design/2026-05-12-ars-v3.7.3-claim-faithfulness-and-contaminated-source-spec.md\u003c/code\u003e. External motivation: Zhao et al. arXiv:2605.07723 (2026-05).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e#108 — AI disclosure policy-anchor renderer\u003c/strong\u003e (audit-trail-shipped 2026-05-14). Adds PRISMA-trAIce / ICMJE / Nature / IEEE policy-anchor disclosure paths alongside the existing venue-track renderer.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e#111 — \u003ccode\u003eslr_lineage\u003c/code\u003e emission on systematic-review → academic-paper handoff\u003c/strong\u003e (2026-05-15). Schema 9 optional boolean \u003ccode\u003eslr_lineage\u003c/code\u003e field; producer \u003ccode\u003epipeline_orchestrator_agent\u003c/code\u003e writes at every handoff transition; consumer \u003ccode\u003edisclosure\u003c/code\u003e mode dispatches \u003ccode\u003e--policy-anchor=prisma-trAIce\u003c/code\u003e per the §4.3 G2 invariant track gate.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e#104 — README motivation: Zhao et al. corpus-scale evidence anchor\u003c/strong\u003e (2026-05-15). README + \u003ccode\u003eREADME.zh-TW.md\u003c/code\u003e motivation section frames the v3.7.x line against Zhao et al.'s 146,932 hallucinated-citation finding.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e#105 — v3.7.3 contamination_signals backfill migration tool\u003c/strong\u003e (2026-05-15). \u003ccode\u003escripts/migrate_literature_corpus_to_v3_7_3.py\u003c/code\u003e retro-computes both contamination signals across pre-v3.7.3 passports.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e#115 — Semantic Scholar client maturity\u003c/strong\u003e (2026-05-15). \u003ccode\u003escripts/semantic_scholar_client.py\u003c/code\u003e adds 1-req/s throttle (drops to 0.1s when \u003ccode\u003eS2_API_KEY\u003c/code\u003e detected), outage latch on URLError, and \u003ccode\u003ereset_outage_latch()\u003c/code\u003e for long-running cross-passport batches.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.7.0 (2026-05-05) — Claude Code Plugin Packaging\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v370-2026-05-05--claude-code-plugin-packaging\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.7.0 (2026-05-05) — Claude Code Plugin Packaging\" href=\"#v370-2026-05-05--claude-code-plugin-packaging\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePlugin packaging upgrade: ARS now installs in one line on Claude Code CLI / VS Code / JetBrains via \u003ccode\u003e/plugin marketplace add Imbad0202/academic-research-skills\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003e/plugin install academic-research-skills\u003c/code\u003e. The traditional \u003ccode\u003egit clone + symlink to ~/.claude/skills/\u003c/code\u003e flow continues to work — both tracks are first-class.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePlugin manifest + marketplace metadata\u003c/strong\u003e (Phase 1, PR #68). \u003ccode\u003e.claude-plugin/plugin.json\u003c/code\u003e declares the suite (4 skills auto-discovered from \u003ccode\u003eskills/\u003c/code\u003e directory via relative symlinks). \u003ccode\u003e.claude-plugin/marketplace.json\u003c/code\u003e registers the plugin so a single GitHub-hosted endpoint serves both the marketplace listing and the plugin source. README + \u003ccode\u003eREADME.zh-TW.md\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003edocs/SETUP.md\u003c/code\u003e carry dual-track install instructions.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e10 slash commands\u003c/strong\u003e at \u003ccode\u003ecommands/ars-*.md\u003c/code\u003e (Phase 2.1, PR #69) mapping \u003ccode\u003eMODE_REGISTRY.md\u003c/code\u003e entries to \u003ccode\u003e/ars-\u0026lt;mode\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e triggers. Model routing is pinned in each command's frontmatter — \u003ccode\u003eopus\u003c/code\u003e for \u003ccode\u003efull\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003erevision-coach\u003c/code\u003e (architectural / review-interpretation depth), \u003ccode\u003esonnet\u003c/code\u003e for the other 8. No Haiku per project policy.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e3 plugin-shipped agents\u003c/strong\u003e at \u003ccode\u003eagents/*_agent.md\u003c/code\u003e (Phase 2.1, PR #69) as relative symlinks to the v3.6.7-hardened downstream agents in \u003ccode\u003edeep-research/agents/\u003c/code\u003e: \u003ccode\u003esynthesis_agent\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eresearch_architect_agent\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ereport_compiler_agent\u003c/code\u003e. Underscore filenames preserved to keep \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_v3_6_7_pattern_protection.py\u003c/code\u003e hard-pinned paths and INV-3 manifest-confined Clause 1 invariant intact. Symlinks (not copies) preserve a single source of truth and prevent the Pattern C3 attack surface that v3.6.7 §6 inversion sweep + INV-1/2/3 lint closes. (Materialized to real byte-identical copies in #413 — relative symlinks break Windows checkouts without \u003ccode\u003ecore.symlinks\u003c/code\u003e and zip-download installs; the single-source guarantee moved to the \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_agents_mirror_sync.py\u003c/code\u003e byte-equality CI lint.)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003emodel: inherit\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e added to those three source agent frontmatters. Inherit chosen over pinning \u003ccode\u003esonnet\u003c/code\u003e so an opus session running ARS full pipeline keeps opus agents (instead of being capped). The user's \u003ccode\u003e~/.claude/hooks/warn-agent-no-model.sh\u003c/code\u003e PreToolUse hook gates Haiku at the dispatching boundary, so \u003ccode\u003einherit\u003c/code\u003e resolves through an already-Haiku-free model.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSessionStart announce hook\u003c/strong\u003e at \u003ccode\u003ehooks/hooks.json\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003escripts/announce-ars-loaded.sh\u003c/code\u003e (Phase 2.2, PR #70). When the plugin loads, the hook injects an \u003ccode\u003eadditionalContext\u003c/code\u003e listing the 10 slash commands, the 3 plugin agents, and a token-budget pointer into the LLM's first turn. \u003ccode\u003estartup\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eclear\u003c/code\u003e source values get the full announce; \u003ccode\u003eresume\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003ecompact\u003c/code\u003e get a one-line ack to avoid burning context. Bash 3.2 compatible — runs on macOS stock \u003ccode\u003e/bin/bash\u003c/code\u003e with no \u003ccode\u003ebrew install bash\u003c/code\u003e requirement.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePhase 2.2 scope reduction\u003c/strong\u003e: a \u003ccode\u003eSubagentStop → run_codex_audit.sh\u003c/code\u003e codex audit hook was scoped out for v3.7.0 due to a contract gap (the SubagentStop payload carries no stage/deliverable info, so the wrapper would have to half-infer required arguments) and an invoker-class boundary (\u003ccode\u003erun_codex_audit.sh\u003c/code\u003e lines 4–7 forbid same-session in-LLM invocation; PostToolUse fires inside the producing session). Real audit-hook integration deferred to a future release when ARS gains a stage/deliverable propagation contract. See \u003ccode\u003edocs/design/2026-04-30-ars-v3.7.0-plugin-packaging-roadmap.md\u003c/code\u003e Update note 2026-05-05 (Phase 2.2 scope reduction).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003edocs/PERFORMANCE.md\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003e.zh-TW.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e gain a \"v3.7.0 Plugin agents and model routing\" subsection explaining the inherit semantics and current 3-agent scope boundary.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCodex review chain across the three PRs\u003c/strong\u003e: 8 inline iterative rounds + 3 fresh PR-level rounds, all converging to 0 P0/P1/P2 findings before merge. The Phase 2.2 fresh PR review caught one P2 (unquoted \u003ccode\u003e${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}\u003c/code\u003e breaking install paths with spaces) that the inline rounds missed — confirms the value of separating implementation review (inline) from contract review (fresh).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat did NOT change\u003c/strong\u003e: the four skill directories, all 25 modes, agent prompts, schema files, and lint contracts. Plugin packaging only adds new top-level surface (\u003ccode\u003ecommands/\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eagents/\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ehooks/\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e.claude-plugin/\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eskills/\u003c/code\u003e symlink dir, three plugin-agent \u003ccode\u003emodel: inherit\u003c/code\u003e frontmatter additions). Existing 4.3k clone-install users see no breaking change.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.6.8 (2026-05-03) — Generator-Evaluator Contract Gate (v3.6.6 spec ship)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v368-2026-05-03--generator-evaluator-contract-gate-v366-spec-ship\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.6.8 (2026-05-03) — Generator-Evaluator Contract Gate (v3.6.6 spec ship)\" href=\"#v368-2026-05-03--generator-evaluator-contract-gate-v366-spec-ship\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNaming note: this release ships the \u003cstrong\u003ev3.6.6 generator-evaluator contract\u003c/strong\u003e spec\nand implementation. The v3.6.6 work landed after v3.6.7 due to project sequencing;\nthe design doc retains the v3.6.6 internal naming for the contract gate version,\nwhile the suite release is tagged v3.6.8 to keep the CHANGELOG monotonic.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSchema 13.1\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eshared/sprint_contract.schema.json\u003c/code\u003e) extends Schema 13 with two new \u003ccode\u003emode\u003c/code\u003e enum values (\u003ccode\u003ewriter_full\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003eevaluator_full\u003c/code\u003e), two new optional top-level fields (\u003ccode\u003epre_commitment_artifacts\u003c/code\u003e writer-only, \u003ccode\u003edisagreement_handling\u003c/code\u003e evaluator-only), and 12 \u003ccode\u003eallOf\u003c/code\u003e branches enforcing reviewer- / writer- / evaluator-conditional gates. Existing reviewer contracts validate byte-equivalent under Schema 13.1 (§3.6 zero-touch promise).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTwo new shipped contract templates\u003c/strong\u003e under \u003ccode\u003eshared/contracts/writer/full.json\u003c/code\u003e (D1–D7, F1/F4/F2/F3/F0) and \u003ccode\u003eshared/contracts/evaluator/full.json\u003c/code\u003e (D1–D5, F1/F2/F3/F6/F4/F5/F0). Promoted from design-time artefacts on the spec branch to live shipped status atomically with the Schema 13.1 upgrade.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTwo-phase orchestration\u003c/strong\u003e inside \u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper full\u003c/code\u003e: Phase 4 splits into Phase 4a (writer paper-blind pre-commitment) + Phase 4b (writer paper-visible drafting + self-scoring); Phase 6 splits into Phase 6a (evaluator paper-blind pre-commitment) + Phase 6b (evaluator paper-visible scoring + decision). Phase-numbered \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;phase4a_output\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;phase6a_output\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e data delimiters mirror the v3.6.2 reviewer pattern. Lint count summary: writer 3+4 / evaluator 5+5 / reviewer 5+6 (reviewer remains zero-touch).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper\u003c/code\u003e SKILL + agent files\u003c/strong\u003e gain a verbatim \u003ccode\u003e## v3.6.6 Generator-Evaluator Contract Protocol\u003c/code\u003e block (101 lines in SKILL.md plus 47 lines in \u003ccode\u003edraft_writer_agent.md\u003c/code\u003e + 57 lines in \u003ccode\u003epeer_reviewer_agent.md\u003c/code\u003e). SKILL.md also adds a new \u003ccode\u003e## Known limitations\u003c/code\u003e section carrying graceful-degradation + cross-session resume forward notes for v3.6.7+.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eValidator extensions\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_sprint_contract.py\u003c/code\u003e SC-* mode-gating audit (SC-5 + SC-11 reviewer-only; SC-9 extended across all three mode families). 17 new tests bring the validator unit-test count from 54 to 71 (positive + 5 schema-branch negative + 2 §3.6 reviewer regression + 6 mode-gating tests).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eManifest CI lint\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_v3_6_6_ab_manifest.py\u003c/code\u003e enforces §6.2 manifest schema + §6.5 git-tracked invariants on \u003ccode\u003etests/fixtures/v3.6.6-ab/manifest.yaml\u003c/code\u003e. \u003ccode\u003e.github/workflows/spec-consistency.yml\u003c/code\u003e extends the sprint contract validation loop to iterate writer + evaluator template directories alongside the existing reviewer loop, plus runs the new manifest CI lint.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eA/B evidence fixture stub\u003c/strong\u003e at \u003ccode\u003etests/fixtures/v3.6.6-ab/\u003c/code\u003e (30 files): manifest + README + 6 paper-A inputs/baseline + 1 paper-C inputs/baseline + Stage 3 reviewer excerpt + 6 codex-judge baseline placeholders. Real fixture data populates in follow-up commits before the implementation work fully completes.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.6.7 (2026-04-30) — Downstream-Agent Pattern Protection (Step 1+2)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v367-2026-04-30--downstream-agent-pattern-protection-step-12\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.6.7 (2026-04-30) — Downstream-Agent Pattern Protection (Step 1+2)\" href=\"#v367-2026-04-30--downstream-agent-pattern-protection-step-12\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThree downstream agents hardened against 13 of 17 documented hallucination/drift patterns\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003esynthesis_agent\u003c/code\u003e (A1–A5 narrative-side), the survey-designer mode of \u003ccode\u003eresearch_architect_agent\u003c/code\u003e (B1–B5 instrument-side), and the abstract-only mode of \u003ccode\u003ereport_compiler_agent\u003c/code\u003e (C1–C3 publication-side). Each agent prompt now carries a \u003ccode\u003ePATTERN PROTECTION (v3.6.7)\u003c/code\u003e block.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFour reference files in \u003ccode\u003eshared/references/\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003eirb_terminology_glossary.md\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003epsychometric_terminology_glossary.md\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eprotected_hedging_phrases.md\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eword_count_conventions.md\u003c/code\u003e. The reference files carry operational contracts that the agent prompts cite by path.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCross-model audit prompt template\u003c/strong\u003e at \u003ccode\u003eshared/templates/codex_audit_multifile_template.md\u003c/code\u003e with seven audit dimensions and a mandatory three-part Section 4(f) check for \u003ccode\u003ereport_compiler_agent\u003c/code\u003e bundles. Failure of any sub-check is a P1 finding.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStatic lint + 29-test mutation suite\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_v3_6_7_pattern_protection.py\u003c/code\u003e enforces protection-clause presence and obligation-phrase shape; \u003ccode\u003escripts/test_check_v3_6_7_pattern_protection.py\u003c/code\u003e preserves codex review evidence so future checker regressions surface in CI. Both are wired into \u003ccode\u003e.github/workflows/spec-consistency.yml\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCodex review history\u003c/strong\u003e: seven rounds of \u003ccode\u003egpt-5.5\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003exhigh\u003c/code\u003e cross-model review reached SHIP-OK with zero P1+P2 findings. Step 6 (orchestrator runtime hooks) and Step 8 (synthetic eval case) ship in a follow-up PR.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.6.5 (2026-04-27) — Material Passport \u003ccode\u003eliterature_corpus[]\u003c/code\u003e Consumer Integration\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v365-2026-04-27--material-passport-literature_corpus-consumer-integration\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.6.5 (2026-04-27) — Material Passport literature_corpus[] Consumer Integration\" href=\"#v365-2026-04-27--material-passport-literature_corpus-consumer-integration\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTwo Phase 1 literature consumers\u003c/strong\u003e wired: \u003ccode\u003edeep-research/agents/bibliography_agent.md\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper/agents/literature_strategist_agent.md\u003c/code\u003e. Both follow the same five-step \u003cstrong\u003ecorpus-first, search-fills-gap\u003c/strong\u003e flow when the passport carries a non-empty \u003ccode\u003eliterature_corpus[]\u003c/code\u003e and the same four Iron Rules (Same criteria / No silent skip / No corpus mutation / Graceful fallback on parse failure).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePRE-SCREENED reproducibility block\u003c/strong\u003e in Search Strategy reports: enumerates included / excluded / skipped corpus entries, with F3 zero-hit note and F4a–F4f provenance reporting that compose around partial declaration of \u003ccode\u003eobtained_via\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eobtained_at\u003c/code\u003e. \u003ccode\u003efinal_included = pre_screened_included[] ∪ external_included[]\u003c/code\u003e stays neutral — no provenance tags on bibliography entries or literature matrix rows.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eConsumer protocol reference\u003c/strong\u003e at \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline/references/literature_corpus_consumers.md\u003c/code\u003e with the canonical PRE-SCREENED template, BAD/GOOD examples, four Iron Rules, and per-consumer reading instructions.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCI lint\u003c/strong\u003e \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_corpus_consumer_protocol.py\u003c/code\u003e enforcing nine protocol invariants with manifest-driven consumer list (\u003ccode\u003escripts/corpus_consumer_manifest.json\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSchema 9 caveat retired\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003eshared/handoff_schemas.md\u003c/code\u003e retired the v3.6.4 \"Consumer-side integration deferred to v3.6.5+\" caveat; replaced with backpointer to the consumer protocol.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003ePresence-based, no schema change, no new env flag. Parse failures fall back to external-DB-only flow with a \u003ccode\u003e[CORPUS PARSE FAILURE]\u003c/code\u003e surface. \u003ccode\u003ecitation_compliance_agent\u003c/code\u003e corpus integration deferred (target version TBD post-v3.8).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNo breaking changes. Existing user adapters work without modification.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.6.4 (2026-04-25) — Material Passport \u003ccode\u003eliterature_corpus[]\u003c/code\u003e Input Port\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v364-2026-04-25--material-passport-literature_corpus-input-port\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.6.4 (2026-04-25) — Material Passport literature_corpus[] Input Port\" href=\"#v364-2026-04-25--material-passport-literature_corpus-input-port\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003eliterature_corpus[]\u003c/code\u003e field\u003c/strong\u003e added to Schema 9 as an optional input port for user-owned literature. Each entry conforms to \u003ccode\u003eshared/contracts/passport/literature_corpus_entry.schema.json\u003c/code\u003e (CSL-JSON authors, year, title, source_pointer + private optional \u003ccode\u003eabstract\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003euser_notes\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLanguage-neutral adapter contract\u003c/strong\u003e at \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline/references/adapters/overview.md\u003c/code\u003e: any program (any language) reading a user corpus source can produce conformant \u003ccode\u003epassport.yaml\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003erejection_log.yaml\u003c/code\u003e. Fail-soft entry-level errors, fail-loud adapter-level errors, deterministic ordering.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThree reference Python adapters\u003c/strong\u003e under \u003ccode\u003escripts/adapters/\u003c/code\u003e: \u003ccode\u003efolder_scan.py\u003c/code\u003e (filesystem of PDFs), \u003ccode\u003ezotero.py\u003c/code\u003e (Better BibTeX JSON export), \u003ccode\u003eobsidian.py\u003c/code\u003e (vault frontmatter). Starting points only; users are expected to write their own adapters for non-reference sources.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRejection log contract\u003c/strong\u003e at \u003ccode\u003eshared/contracts/passport/rejection_log.schema.json\u003c/code\u003e with closed enum of categorical reason values; always emitted (empty when no rejections).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCI gates\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_literature_corpus_schema.py\u003c/code\u003e validates schemas + adapter examples; \u003ccode\u003escripts/sync_adapter_docs.py --check\u003c/code\u003e prevents schema→docs drift; new \u003ccode\u003epytest.yml\u003c/code\u003e workflow runs \u003ccode\u003escripts/adapters/tests/\u003c/code\u003e on path-filtered triggers.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInput-port-only at v3.6.4\u003c/strong\u003e: v3.6.4 shipped the schema and adapter contract without consumer integration. \u003ccode\u003ebibliography_agent\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eliterature_strategist_agent\u003c/code\u003e were wired in v3.6.5.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNo breaking changes.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.6.3 (2026-04-23) — Opt-in Passport Reset Boundary\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v363-2026-04-23--opt-in-passport-reset-boundary\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.6.3 (2026-04-23) — Opt-in Passport Reset Boundary\" href=\"#v363-2026-04-23--opt-in-passport-reset-boundary\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOpt-in passport reset boundary\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eARS_PASSPORT_RESET=1\u003c/code\u003e). Promotes every FULL checkpoint to a context-reset boundary. New \u003ccode\u003eresume_from_passport=\u0026lt;hash\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e mode lets users resume in a fresh Claude Code session from the Material Passport ledger alone. \u003ccode\u003esystematic-review\u003c/code\u003e mode with the flag ON makes reset mandatory at every FULL checkpoint; other modes treat reset as the flag-gated default. Flag OFF preserves pre-v3.6.3 behavior byte-for-byte.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSchema 9 gains an append-only \u003ccode\u003ereset_boundary[]\u003c/code\u003e ledger with two entry kinds (\u003ccode\u003ekind: boundary\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003ekind: resume\u003c/code\u003e). Hash uses JSON Canonical Form + SHA-256 with canonical placeholder for self-reference safety. Optional \u003ccode\u003epending_decision\u003c/code\u003e handles MANDATORY branch choices.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNew \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_passport_reset_contract.py\u003c/code\u003e CI lint: every mention of the flag must co-locate a pointer to the authoritative protocol doc.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eProtocol doc: \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline/references/passport_as_reset_boundary.md\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003edocs/PERFORMANCE.md\u003c/code\u003e updated with long-running-session guidance.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNo breaking changes. Flag default is OFF.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.6.2 (2026-04-23) — Reviewer Sprint Contract Hard Gate\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v362-2026-04-23--reviewer-sprint-contract-hard-gate\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.6.2 (2026-04-23) — Reviewer Sprint Contract Hard Gate\" href=\"#v362-2026-04-23--reviewer-sprint-contract-hard-gate\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.6.2 introduces Schema 13 sprint contracts and a hard-gate orchestration that forces reviewers to pre-commit their scoring plan before reading the paper. Reviewer-only first test case; writer/evaluator deferred to v3.6.4. See CHANGELOG.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSchema 13 sprint contract\u003c/strong\u003e with \u003ccode\u003epanel_size\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eacceptance_dimensions\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003efailure_conditions\u003c/code\u003e (with \u003ccode\u003eseverity\u003c/code\u003e precedence + panel-relative \u003ccode\u003ecross_reviewer_quantifier\u003c/code\u003e), \u003ccode\u003emeasurement_procedure\u003c/code\u003e, optional \u003ccode\u003eoverride_ladder\u003c/code\u003e, bounded \u003ccode\u003eagent_amendments\u003c/code\u003e. Validator: \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_sprint_contract.py\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTwo-call hard gate.\u003c/strong\u003e Reviewers run paper-content-blind Phase 1 + paper-visible Phase 2; Phase 1 output is wrapped in \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;phase1_output\u0026gt;...\u0026lt;/phase1_output\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e data delimiter to narrow the self-injection surface.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSynthesizer three-step mechanical protocol.\u003c/strong\u003e Build cross-reviewer matrix → evaluate each \u003ccode\u003efailure_condition\u003c/code\u003e with panel-relative quantifier + recognised expression vocabulary → resolve precedence by \u003ccode\u003eseverity\u003c/code\u003e. Forbidden-ops list explicit in \u003ccode\u003eeditorial_synthesizer_agent\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTwo reviewer templates ship\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eshared/contracts/reviewer/full.json\u003c/code\u003e panel 5; \u003ccode\u003eshared/contracts/reviewer/methodology_focus.json\u003c/code\u003e panel 2). \u003ccode\u003ereviewer_re_review\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ereviewer_calibration\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ereviewer_guided\u003c/code\u003e are reserved in the schema enum but ship without contract templates in v3.6.2; they retain pre-v3.6.2 behaviour. \u003ccode\u003ereviewer_quick\u003c/code\u003e is excluded from the enum entirely.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper-reviewer\u003c/code\u003e SKILL version: \u003ccode\u003e1.8.1 → 1.9.0\u003c/code\u003e. \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e SKILL version: \u003ccode\u003e3.5.1 → 3.6.2\u003c/code\u003e (suite-version invariant). Suite version bumped to \u003ccode\u003e3.6.2\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSee spec \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/docs/design/2026-04-23-ars-v3.6.2-sprint-contract-design.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003edocs/design/2026-04-23-ars-v3.6.2-sprint-contract-design.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e and protocol \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/academic-paper-reviewer/references/sprint_contract_protocol.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper-reviewer/references/sprint_contract_protocol.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.5.1 (2026-04-22) — Opt-in Socratic Reading-Check Probe\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v351-2026-04-22--opt-in-socratic-reading-check-probe\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.5.1 (2026-04-22) — Opt-in Socratic Reading-Check Probe\" href=\"#v351-2026-04-22--opt-in-socratic-reading-check-probe\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.5.1 adds an opt-in honesty probe to the Socratic Mentor (\u003ccode\u003eARS_SOCRATIC_READING_PROBE=1\u003c/code\u003e). Default off. See CHANGELOG.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOpt-in reading-check probe\u003c/strong\u003e: when \u003ccode\u003eARS_SOCRATIC_READING_PROBE=1\u003c/code\u003e is set, the Socratic Mentor fires a one-time honesty probe during goal-oriented sessions where the user has cited a specific paper. Decline is logged without penalty. Outcome flows into the Research Plan Summary and Stage 6 AI Self-Reflection Report. No new agent, no schema change.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003edeep-research\u003c/code\u003e SKILL version: \u003ccode\u003e2.9.0 → 2.9.1\u003c/code\u003e. \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e SKILL version: \u003ccode\u003e3.5.0 → 3.5.1\u003c/code\u003e. Suite version bumped to \u003ccode\u003e3.5.1\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.5.0 (2026-04-21) — Collaboration Depth Observer\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v350-2026-04-21--collaboration-depth-observer\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.5.0 (2026-04-21) — Collaboration Depth Observer\" href=\"#v350-2026-04-21--collaboration-depth-observer\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNew agent\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ccode\u003ecollaboration_depth_agent\u003c/code\u003e in \u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e (Agent Team grows from 3 to 4). Invoked at every FULL/SLIM checkpoint and at pipeline completion; scores user-AI collaboration against a 4-dimension rubric. \u003cstrong\u003eAdvisory only — never blocks progression.\u003c/strong\u003e MANDATORY checkpoints (Stages 2.5 / 4.5 integrity gates) do NOT invoke the observer.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNew rubric\u003c/strong\u003e: \u003ca href=\"/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/collaboration_depth_rubric.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eshared/collaboration_depth_rubric.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e v1.0. Dimensions: Delegation Intensity, Cognitive Vigilance, Cognitive Reallocation, Zone Classification (Zone 1 / Zone 2 / Zone 3). Based on Wang, S., \u0026amp; Zhang, H. (2026). \"Pedagogical partnerships with generative AI in higher education: how dual cognitive pathways paradoxically enable transformative learning.\" \u003cem\u003eInternational Journal of Educational Technology in Higher Education\u003c/em\u003e, 23:11. DOI \u003ca href=\"https://doi.org/10.1186/s41239-026-00585-x\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003e10.1186/s41239-026-00585-x\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCross-model divergence flagged, not averaged\u003c/strong\u003e: when \u003ccode\u003eARS_CROSS_MODEL\u003c/code\u003e is set the observer runs on both models; dimension disagreement \u0026gt; 2 points is reported rather than silently smoothed. \u003ccode\u003eARS_CROSS_MODEL_SAMPLE_INTERVAL\u003c/code\u003e escape hatch for cost trade-off.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eShort-stage guard\u003c/strong\u003e: stages with fewer than 5 user turns inject a static \u003ccode\u003einsufficient_evidence\u003c/code\u003e block instead of dispatching the full-model observer.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAnti-sycophancy discipline\u003c/strong\u003e: scores ≥ 7 require specific dialogue-turn citations; Zone 3 triggers re-audit; no motivational framing.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eacademic-pipeline\u003c/code\u003e SKILL version: \u003ccode\u003e3.3.0 → 3.4.0\u003c/code\u003e. Suite version bumped to \u003ccode\u003e3.5.0\u003c/code\u003e. New lint \u003ccode\u003escripts/check_collaboration_depth_rubric.py\u003c/code\u003e + 10 tests.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.4.0 (2026-04-20) — Compliance Agent + Schema 12\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v340-2026-04-20--compliance-agent--schema-12\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.4.0 (2026-04-20) — Compliance Agent + Schema 12\" href=\"#v340-2026-04-20--compliance-agent--schema-12\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCompliance Agent\u003c/strong\u003e (shared): single mode-aware agent running PRISMA-trAIce 17 items (SR mode only) + RAISE 4 principles + 8-role matrix. Hooks existing Stage 2.5 / 4.5 Integrity Gates; tier-based block (Mandatory → block, HR → warn, R/O → info). Non-SR entries run principles-only, warn-only.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSchema 12 compliance_report\u003c/strong\u003e appended to Material Passport via \u003ccode\u003ecompliance_history[]\u003c/code\u003e (append-only).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e3-round user-override ladder\u003c/strong\u003e auto-injects \u003ccode\u003edisclosure_addendum\u003c/code\u003e into manuscript. No detection evasion possible.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCalibration with transparent reporting\u003c/strong\u003e, no hard FNR/FPR gate — self-consistent with \u003ccode\u003etask_type: open-ended\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUpstream freshness CI\u003c/strong\u003e warns on PRISMA-trAIce drift (non-blocking).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLong-running session docs\u003c/strong\u003e: Material Passport as cross-session resume mechanism.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.3.6 (2026-04-15) — README rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ePaperOrchestra\u003c/a\u003e (Song, Song, Pfister \u0026amp; Yoon, 2026, Google).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSemantic Scholar API Verification\u003c/strong\u003e — Tier 0 programmatic reference existence check via S2 API. Levenshtein \u0026gt;= 0.70 title matching, DOI mismatch detection, bibliography deduplication via S2 IDs. Graceful degradation if API unavailable.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAnti-Leakage Protocol\u003c/strong\u003e — Knowledge Isolation Directive prioritizes session materials over LLM parametric memory. Flags \u003ccode\u003e[MATERIAL GAP]\u003c/code\u003e for missing content instead of filling from memory. Reduces Mode 5/6 failure risk.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVLM Figure Verification\u003c/strong\u003e (optional) — Closed-loop verification of rendered figures using vision-capable LLM. 10-point checklist, max 2 refinement iterations.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eScore Trajectory Protocol\u003c/strong\u003e — Per-dimension rubric score delta tracking across revision rounds (7 dimensions). Detects regressions (delta \u0026lt; -3) and triggers mandatory checkpoint.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStage 2 Parallelization\u003c/strong\u003e — Visualization and argument building can run in parallel after outline completion.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNew versions: deep-research v2.8, academic-paper v3.0, academic-pipeline v3.2\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ev3.2 (2026-04-09) — Lu 2026 Nature Integration\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-v32-2026-04-09--lu-2026-nature-integration\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: v3.2 (2026-04-09) — Lu 2026 Nature Integration\" href=\"#v32-2026-04-09--lu-2026-nature-integration\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIntegrates insights from Lu et al. Threshold\u003c/strong\u003e (deep-research + academic-paper-reviewer): DA must score rebuttals 1-5 before responding. Concession only at ≥4. No consecutive concessions. Concession rate tracking. Frame-lock detection after each checkpoint.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAttack Intensity Preservation\u003c/strong\u003e (academic-paper-reviewer): DA does not soften under pushback. Rebuttal assessment protocol with explicit deflection detection. Anti-sycophancy rules prevent persistent pushback from being treated as valid evidence.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIntent Detection Layer\u003c/strong\u003e (deep-research socratic): Classifies user intent as exploratory vs. goal-oriented. Exploratory mode disables auto-convergence, raises max rounds, prohibits premature closure. Re-assesses every 3 turns.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDialogue Health Indicator\u003c/strong\u003e (deep-research socratic): Silent self-check every 5 turns for persistent agreement, conflict avoidance, premature convergence. Auto-injects challenges when agreement pattern detected.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCross-Model Verification Protocol\u003c/strong\u003e (shared, optional): Use GPT-5.4 Pro or Gemini 3.1 Pro for integrity verification sample cross-checks and independent DA critique. Sixth-reviewer peer review remains planned, not yet implemented. Activated by setting \u003ccode\u003eARS_CROSS_MODEL\u003c/code\u003e env var — without it, everything works as before. See \u003ccode\u003eshared/cross_model_verification.md\u003c/code\u003e for full setup guide, API patterns, and cost estimates.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAI Self-Reflection Report\u003c/strong\u003e (academic-pipeline Stage 6): Post-pipeline self-assessment of AI behavioral patterns — DA concession rate, checkpoint skip rate, health alerts, sycophancy risk rating (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH), frame-lock incidents, convergence pattern analysis. Includes irony caveat: \"this self-reflection is itself produced by the same AI that may have been sycophantic.\"\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOrigin: Discovered through a 4-round dialectic experiment where the DA conceded too quickly, the Socratic Mentor tried to converge prematurely, and the entire debate stayed locked in a frame the human set.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eVersions: deep-research v2.5, academic-paper-reviewer v1.5, academic-pipeline v2.8\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStyle Calibration\u003c/strong\u003e (academic-paper intake Step 10, optional): Provide 3+ past papers and the pipeline learns your writing voice — sentence rhythm, vocabulary preferences, citation integration style. Applied as a soft guide during drafting; discipline conventions always take priority. Priority system: discipline norms (hard) \u0026gt; journal conventions (strong) \u0026gt; personal style (soft). See \u003ccode\u003eshared/style_calibration_protocol.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWriting Quality Check\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eacademic-paper/references/writing_quality_check.md\u003c/code\u003e): Writing quality checklist applied during draft self-review. 5 categories: AI high-frequency term warnings (25 terms), punctuation pattern control (em dash ≤3), throat-clearing opener detection, structural pattern warnings (Rule of Three, uniform paragraphs, synonym cycling), and burstiness checks (sentence length variation). These are good writing rules — not detection evasion\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStyle Profile\u003c/strong\u003e carried through academic-pipeline Material Passport (Schema 10 in \u003ccode\u003eshared/handoff_schemas.md\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edeep-research\u003c/strong\u003e report compiler also consumes both features optionally\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eVersions: academic-paper v2.5, deep-research v2.4, academic-pipeline v2.7\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" first.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTwo-layer architecture: Layer 1 (skill activation) uses bilingual keywords for matching confidence; Layer 2 (mode routing) uses language-agnostic intent <p dir="auto">A comprehensive suite of Claude Code skills for academic research, covering the full pipeline from research to publication.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Install in 30 seconds</strong> (Claude Code CLI / VS Code / JetBrains, v3.7.0+):</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre lang="text" class="notranslate"><code>/plugin marketplace add Imbad0202/academic-research-skills
/plugin install academic-research-skills
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Then try <code>/ars-plan</code> to walk through your paper structure via Socratic dialogue, or jump to <a href="#quick-install">Quick install</a> for prerequisites and the traditional symlink flow.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>AI is your copilot, not the pilot.</strong> This tool won't write your paper for you. It handles the grunt work — hunting down references, formatting citations, verifying data, checking logical consistency — so you can focus on the parts that actually require your brain: defining the question, choosing the method, interpreting what the data means, and writing the sentence after "I argue that."</p>
<p dir="auto">Unlike a humanizer, this tool doesn't help you hide the fact that you used AI. It helps you write better. Style Calibration learns your voice from past work. Writing Quality Check catches the patterns that make prose feel machine-generated. The goal is quality, not cheating.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why human-in-the-loop, not full automation?</h3><a id="user-content-why-human-in-the-loop-not-full-automation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why human-in-the-loop, not full automation?" href="#why-human-in-the-loop-not-full-automation"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Lu et al. (2026, <em>Nature</em> 651:914-919) built <strong>The AI Scientist</strong> — the first fully autonomous AI research system to publish a paper through blind peer review at a top-tier ML venue (ICLR 2025 workshop, score 6.33/10 vs workshop average 4.87). Their Limitations section enumerates the failure modes that any fully-autonomous AI research pipeline inherits: implementation bugs, hallucinated results, shortcut reliance, bug-as-insight reframing, methodology fabrication, frame-lock, citation hallucinations.</p>
<p dir="auto">ARS is built on the premise that <strong>a human researcher augmented by AI avoids these failure modes better than either alone</strong>. Stage 2.5 and Stage 4.5 integrity gates run a 7-mode blocking checklist (see <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/academic-pipeline/references/ai_research_failure_modes.md"><code>academic-pipeline/references/ai_research_failure_modes.md</code></a>); the reviewer offers an opt-in calibration mode that measures its own FNR/FPR against a user-supplied gold set.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.07723" rel="nofollow"><strong>Zhao et al.</strong></a> (2026-05) audited 111M references across 2.5M papers on arXiv, bioRxiv, SSRN, and PMC. Their conservative estimate is 146,932 hallucinated citations for 2025 alone, with an observed mid-2024 inflection; for the bioRxiv-to-PMC pairing they report 85.3% preprint-to-published persistence. The paper describes "real citations deployed to support claims the cited references do not actually make" as an open challenge. ARS v3.7.1 added trust-chain frontmatter for source provenance; v3.7.3 added locator infrastructure (three-layer citation anchors) for future claim-level audits and surfaces advisory risk signals at cite time (ARS labels the claim-faithfulness gap internally as "L3"; this is ARS terminology, not the paper's). v3.7.x is motivated by Zhao et al.'s corpus-scale findings; corpus-scale evaluation of ARS itself remains future work.</p>
<p dir="auto">v3.8 closes the second half of the L3 gap. v3.7.3 made every citation carry a locator anchor; v3.8 adds an opt-in audit pass (<code>ARS_CLAIM_AUDIT=1</code>) that fetches the cited source against each anchor and judges whether the claim is actually supported. Five new HIGH-WARN classes (claim-not-supported, negative-constraint-violation, fabricated-reference, anchorless, constraint-violation-uncited) gate-refuse output through the formatter terminal hard gate. Calibration is shipped as a 20-tuple gold set with FNR<0.15 + FPR<0.10 acceptance thresholds; ramp-on plan is deferred to post-calibration evidence per v3.8 spec §5.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.13104" rel="nofollow"><strong>Ren et al.</strong></a> (2026, <em>Self-Improvements in Modern Agentic Systems: A Survey</em>) supplies a third, survey-level anchor. Its scientific-discovery synthesis (§7.4) concludes that discovery agents cannot easily verify novelty, correctness, or reproducibility on their own and may exploit weak proxies instead, must manage evidence across heterogeneous tools and literature, and raise governance issues — "scientific writing can also amplify misinformation when the evidence is weak." Its generation-loop chapters (§5.1–§5.2) list human auditing and retained human anchors among the practical safeguards for self-generated evaluation loops, and its historical chapter (§2.2) records the oldest form of the same lesson: the practical success of Lenat's EURISKO depended heavily on the user serving as the external evaluation signal, pruning unproductive heuristic drift — a limitation the survey notes persists in modern agentic systems. ARS cites the survey as design rationale for its human-in-the-loop stance, not as empirical proof that human-in-the-loop pipelines outperform autonomous ones; the survey's actionable deltas for ARS are tracked in #539–#541 and #547–#550.</p>
<p dir="auto">v3.3 was inspired by <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05018" rel="nofollow"><strong>PaperOrchestra</strong></a> (Song, Song, Pfister & Yoon, 2026, Google): Semantic Scholar API verification, anti-leakage protocol, VLM figure verification, and score trajectory tracking.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Architecture & pipeline</h2><a id="user-content-architecture--pipeline" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Architecture & pipeline" href="#architecture--pipeline"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>👉 <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md">docs/ARCHITECTURE.md</a></strong> — the full pipeline view: flow diagram, stage-by-stage matrix, data-access flow, skill dependency graph, quality gates, and mode list.</p>
<p dir="auto">The architecture doc supersedes the sprawling pipeline description that used to live here. Everything about <em>what runs in which stage</em> now lives in one place.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Quick install</h2><a id="user-content-quick-install" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Quick install" href="#quick-install"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Prerequisites</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/setup" rel="nofollow">Claude Code</a> (latest; plugin packaging requires recent versions)</li>
<li><code>ANTHROPIC_API_KEY</code> exported, or set on first <code>claude</code> run</li>
<li><em>Optional:</em> Pandoc for DOCX, tectonic + Source Han Serif TC for APA 7.0 PDF (Markdown output works without either)</li>
<li><em>Optional (real Python):</em> The core skills (research / write / review) need no Python — they are prompt-driven. A <strong>real Python interpreter</strong> is needed only for: the <code>PreToolUse</code> write-scope guard (optional subagent hardening — if no real Python is found it cleanly no-ops and the guard is simply inactive; core skills are unaffected), plus a few opt-in features that shell out to Python (revision-patch mode, the submission-package verifier, and the <code>/ars-cache-invalidate</code> / <code>/ars-mark-read</code> / <code>/ars-unmark-read</code> commands). On Windows, note that <code>python3</code> is often a non-functional Microsoft Store placeholder rather than real Python; install Python from python.org (or via <code>winget</code>) so the launcher can find a real interpreter. The guard launcher is a POSIX shell script and <code>hooks.json</code> invokes it through <code>bash</code>, so on Windows it needs <strong>Git Bash</strong> (bundled with Git for Windows). With Git Bash present, a missing real Python degrades cleanly (the guard no-ops, silently). Without Git Bash, Claude Code falls back to PowerShell, which cannot run the <code>.sh</code> launcher at all: the guard is inactive and the <code>PreToolUse</code> hook will log an error per call rather than no-op quietly (accepted degradation — the guard is optional and never blocks your writes, but the hook noise is the trade-off until Git Bash is installed).</li>
</ul>

<p dir="auto"><strong>👉 <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/docs/SETUP.md">docs/SETUP.md</a></strong> — full guide: install Claude Code, set up API keys, optional Pandoc/tectonic for DOCX/PDF, cross-model verification (<code>ARS_CROSS_MODEL</code>), and six installation methods (Plugin, project skills, global skills, claude.ai Project, repo-cloned, Claude Science import).</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Using Claude Science?</strong> The four skills import directly: <strong>Skills → Import from GitHub</strong>, paste <code>https://github.com/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills</code>, <strong>Preview</strong>, then <strong>Import 4 skills</strong> (requires v3.14.0+ of this repo — the importer reads the explicit skill paths in the marketplace manifest). Imports are point-in-time snapshots: re-import after ARS updates. Imported skills carry the ARS methodology (research / writing / review protocols); Claude Code-specific machinery — slash commands, hooks, subagent orchestration — does not transfer. See <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/docs/SETUP.md">docs/SETUP.md</a> Method 5 for details.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Using Codex CLI?</strong> Install the sibling distribution instead: <a href="https://github.com/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills-codex"><code>Imbad0202/academic-research-skills-codex</code></a> — same workflow content, Codex-native packaging as a single <code>$academic-research-suite</code> skill with <code>ars-*</code> aliases.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Features at a glance</h2><a id="user-content-features-at-a-glance" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Features at a glance" href="#features-at-a-glance"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Deep Research</strong> — 13-agent research team with Socratic guided mode, PRISMA systematic review, intent detection, dialogue health monitoring, optional cross-model DA, Semantic Scholar API verification.</li>
<li><strong>Academic Paper</strong> — 12-agent paper writing with Style Calibration, Writing Quality Check, LaTeX hardening, visualization, revision coaching, citation conversion, anti-leakage protocol, and VLM figure verification.</li>
<li><strong>Academic Paper Reviewer</strong> — 7-agent multi-perspective peer review with 0–100 quality rubrics (EIC + 3 dynamic reviewers + Devil's Advocate), concession threshold protocol, attack intensity preservation, optional cross-model DA critique / calibration, R&R traceability matrix, read-only constraint.</li>
<li><strong>Academic Pipeline</strong> — 10-stage pipeline orchestrator with adaptive checkpoints, claim verification, Material Passport, optional <code>repro_lock</code>, optional cross-model integrity verification, mid-conversation reinforcement, and score trajectory tracking.</li>
<li><strong>Data Access Level Metadata</strong> (v3.3.2+) — every skill declares <code>data_access_level</code> (<code>raw</code> / <code>redacted</code> / <code>verified_only</code>); enforced by <code>scripts/check_data_access_level.py</code>. Pattern adapted from Anthropic's automated-w2s-researcher (2026). See <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/ground_truth_isolation_pattern.md"><code>shared/ground_truth_isolation_pattern.md</code></a>.</li>
<li><strong>Task Type Annotation</strong> (v3.3.2+) — every skill declares <code>task_type</code> (<code>open-ended</code> or <code>outcome-gradable</code>). All current ARS skills are <code>open-ended</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Benchmark Report Schema</strong> (v3.3.5+) — JSON Schema + lint for honest benchmark comparisons. See <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/benchmark_report_pattern.md"><code>shared/benchmark_report_pattern.md</code></a>.</li>
<li><strong>Artifact Reproducibility Lockfile</strong> (v3.3.5+) — optional <code>repro_lock</code> sub-block on Material Passport. <strong>Configuration documentation, not replay guarantee</strong> — LLM outputs are not byte-reproducible. See <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/artifact_reproducibility_pattern.md"><code>shared/artifact_reproducibility_pattern.md</code></a>.</li>
<li><strong>Model Tiering</strong> (#517, v3.16+) — optional <code>ARS_MODEL_TIERING</code> switch with two directions: <code>economy</code> (execution-type agents dispatch one tier below the session model, floor Opus-class) and <code>quality-boost</code> (judgment-type agents at integrity gates and final review step up to the frontier tier). Default unset = byte-equivalent to pre-#517 behavior. See <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/model_tiering.md"><code>shared/model_tiering.md</code></a>.</li>
<li><strong>Canonical Cross-Model Handoff Envelope</strong> (#527, v3.17+) — the owner→dispatcher→owner blind-checkpoint transport path (#523) now has a machine-stable <code>[CROSS-MODEL-HANDOFF v1]</code> envelope with a normative Python grammar (<code>scripts/cross_model_handoff.py</code>) instead of prose-only enforcement, pinning agreement/divergence/malformed-result routing across all three checkpoint owners. See <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/cross_model_verification.md"><code>shared/cross_model_verification.md</code></a> §"Cross-model handoff envelope".</li>
<li><strong>Experiment Provenance Intake</strong> (#260) — optional <code>experiment_provenance[]</code> on the Material Passport records experiments the scholar ran <strong>externally</strong> (ARS never runs experiments), and manuscript claims join to them via <code>claim_intent_manifest.planned_experiment_ids[]</code>. The integrity gate (Stage 2.5/4.5) audits each experiment-backed claim against declared provenance — <code>ALIGNED</code> / <code>OVERSTATED</code> / <code>NOT_SUPPORTED_BY_PROVENANCE</code> / <code>PROVENANCE_INSUFFICIENT</code> — <strong>without judging whether the experiment itself was correct</strong>. A fail-closed <code>experiment_intake_declaration</code> makes "did you run experiments?" an explicit Stage 1 decision (even literature-only runs declare <code>no_experiments_declared</code>). See <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/handoff_schemas.md"><code>shared/handoff_schemas.md</code></a> §"Experiment Provenance Intake (#260)".</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Companion: Experiment Agent</h2><a id="user-content-companion-experiment-agent" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Companion: Experiment Agent" href="#companion-experiment-agent"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If your research involves running experiments (code or human studies) before writing, the <a href="https://github.com/Imbad0202/experiment-agent">Experiment Agent</a> skill fills the gap between ARS Stage 1 (RESEARCH) and Stage 2 (WRITE).</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>ARS Stage 1 RESEARCH  →  RQ Brief + Methodology Blueprint
        ↓
  experiment-agent     →  run/manage experiments → validate results
        ↓
ARS Stage 2 WRITE     →  write paper with verified experiment results
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="ARS Stage 1 RESEARCH  →  RQ Brief + Methodology Blueprint
        ↓

        ↓
ARS Stage 2 WRITE     →  write paper with verified experiment results" tabindex="0" role="button">
