<p dir="auto"><strong>What it does</strong>: executes code experiments (Python, R, etc.) with real-time monitoring, manages human study protocols with IRB ethics checklist, interprets statistics with 11-type fallacy detection, and verifies reproducibility.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>How to use together</strong>: pause the ARS pipeline after Stage 1, run experiments in a separate experiment-agent session, then bring the results (with Material Passport) back to ARS Stage 2. ARS requires zero modification. See the <a href="https://github.com/Imbad0202/experiment-agent">experiment-agent README</a> for setup instructions.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Stage 1 intake declaration (#260)</strong>: at Stage 1, ARS detects whether the run will carry experiment-backed claims and sets a fail-closed <code>experiment_intake_declaration</code> on the Material Passport. If you ran experiments externally, the scholar enters one <code>experiment_provenance[]</code> entry per experiment (<code>experiment_id</code>, nested <code>repro_lock</code>, <code>planned_vs_executed[]</code>, <code>negative_results[]</code>, <code>known_limitations[]</code>) and the declaration is set to <code>experiments_declared</code>; if not, it is set to <code>no_experiments_declared</code>. The declaration is <strong>required on every post-#260 passport</strong> — a run that touches no experiments still declares <code>no_experiments_declared</code>, so the integrity gate can never be silently bypassed by a forgotten provenance block. The <code>experiment_id</code>s are frozen at this intake point; the writers later reference them via <code>planned_experiment_ids[]</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Teaching-side companion</strong>: <a href="https://github.com/YujxZJCN/teaching-skills">Teaching Skills</a> applies the ARS architecture (skill ensembles, shared contracts, staged gates, a Course Passport) to the teaching side of academic life — course design → lessons → assessment → delivery → reflection; its <code>sotl</code> mode hands classroom-inquiry projects off to ARS deep-research / academic-paper for the publication phase.</p>

<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Pipeline ends with <strong>Stage 6: Process Summary</strong> — auto-generates a paper creation process record with 6-dimension Collaboration Quality Evaluation (1–100 scoring).</p>
</blockquote>

<p dir="auto">10-stage orchestrator with integrity verification, two-stage review, Socratic coaching, and collaboration evaluation. Pipeline guarantees: every stage requires user confirmation checkpoint; integrity verification (Stage 2.5 + 4.5) cannot be skipped; R&R Traceability Matrix (Schema 11) independently verifies author revision claims. v3.4 added the Compliance Agent (PRISMA-trAIce + RAISE) at Stage 2.5 / 4.5. v3.5 adds the <strong>Collaboration Depth Observer</strong> (<code>collaboration_depth_agent</code>, advisory only — never blocks) at every FULL/SLIM checkpoint and at pipeline completion. MANDATORY integrity gates (2.5 / 4.5) explicitly skip the observer so compliance checks are not diluted. Based on Wang & Zhang (2026), IJETHE 23:11. Stage-by-stage matrix with agents, artifacts, and gates: see ARCHITECTURE.md §3.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.0 Optimizations: What We Discovered About AI's Structural Limits</h2><a id="user-content-v30-optimizations-what-we-discovered-about-ais-structural-limits" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.0 Optimizations: What We Discovered About AI's Structural Limits" href="#v30-optimizations-what-we-discovered-about-ais-structural-limits"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What happened</h3><a id="user-content-what-happened" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What happened" href="#what-happened"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">While using ARS to write a reflection article about AI in higher education, I ran into three structural problems that no amount of prompt engineering could fix:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Frame-lock</strong>: I asked the AI to run a devil's advocate debate against its own thesis. It did — four rounds, each more refined than the last. But every round stayed inside the frame I'd set. The DA attacked arguments, never premises. It never asked "are we even discussing the right question?" This is the same pattern that caused the 31% citation error rate in v2.7's stress test: the verifying AI and the generating AI share the same cognitive frame.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Sycophancy under pushback</strong>: Every time I challenged the DA's attacks, it conceded too quickly. It retracted findings faster than it launched them. The model's training rewards conversational harmony — so "the user pushed back" was treated as evidence that the attack was wrong, when often it just meant the user was persistent.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Intent misdetection</strong>: The Socratic Mentor kept trying to converge and produce deliverables ("Want me to write this up?") when I was still exploring. It couldn't distinguish "the user wants a deep philosophical discussion" from "the user wants an RQ brief." Both look like engagement, but they need opposite AI behaviors.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What we changed (v3.0)</h3><a id="user-content-what-we-changed-v30" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What we changed (v3.0)" href="#what-we-changed-v30"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Devil's Advocate — Concession Threshold Protocol</strong> (<code>deep-research</code> + <code>academic-paper-reviewer</code>)</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>DA must now score every rebuttal on a 1-5 scale before responding</li>
<li>Concession only allowed at score ≥4 (rebuttal directly addresses core attack with evidence)</li>
<li>Score ≤3: hold position and restate the original attack</li>
<li>Anti-sycophancy rules: no consecutive concessions, concession rate tracking, frame-lock detection after each checkpoint</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Socratic Mentor — Intent Detection Layer</strong> (<code>deep-research</code>)</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Classifies user intent as exploratory vs. goal-oriented at dialogue start and every 3 turns</li>
<li>Exploratory mode: disables auto-convergence, raises max rounds to 60, prohibits "want me to summarize?" prompts</li>
<li>Goal-oriented mode: standard convergence behavior</li>
<li>Anti-premature-closure rules: in exploratory mode, the user decides when to stop</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Socratic Mentor — Dialogue Health Indicator</strong> (<code>deep-research</code>)</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Silent self-assessment every 5 turns on three dimensions: persistent agreement, conflict avoidance, premature convergence</li>
<li>Auto-injects challenging questions when agreement pattern detected</li>
<li>Invisible to user (to prevent gaming), but log available for post-session review</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why this matters</h3><a id="user-content-why-this-matters" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why this matters" href="#why-this-matters"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">These optimizations don't solve AI's structural limits — they make the limits visible and manageable. The DA will still eventually concede if pushed hard enough. The Socratic Mentor will still have some convergence bias. But now there are explicit checkpoints that slow down the sycophancy, force the DA to justify concessions, and prevent the Mentor from wrapping up before the user is ready.</p>
<p dir="auto">The deeper lesson: AI literacy isn't about learning to use AI as a tool, following ethics rules, or fearing AI risks. It's about engaging AI deeply enough to discover its structural limits yourself — and your own thinking limits in the process.</p>
<hr>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Changelog</h2><a id="user-content-changelog" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Changelog" href="#changelog"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.19.0 (2026-07-22) — Revision-round claim-drift guards, PDF read-integrity preflight, read-scope attestation</h3><a id="user-content-v3190-2026-07-22--revision-round-claim-drift-guards-pdf-read-integrity-preflight-read-scope-attestation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.19.0 (2026-07-22) — Revision-round claim-drift guards, PDF read-integrity preflight, read-scope attestation" href="#v3190-2026-07-22--revision-round-claim-drift-guards-pdf-read-integrity-preflight-read-scope-attestation"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Added:</strong> three advisory-or-opt-in integrity layers plus a launcher fix. <strong>Revision-round claim-drift guards (#569/#570):</strong> a claim-strength ladder ("no silent move along <code>is associated with < predicts < causes</code> without an authorizing roadmap item") wired into revision drafting and a new advisory Phase E6, plus a deterministic numeric/citation token-conservation checker — together they close the epistemic and token halves of the #390 honest-claim residual (a touched block's interior had no fidelity check). Baseline measured on the current frontier model first (<code>evals/heldout/revision_claim_drift/</code>), mechanism shape credited to <a href="https://github.com/Yila-AI/sci-ssci-skills">Yila-AI/sci-ssci-skills</a>. <strong>PDF read-integrity preflight (#512):</strong> a three-signal page-count cross-check so a truncated / mispaginated PDF read cannot mint an apparently-valid <code>page</code> anchor. <strong><code>read_scope</code> attestation (#513):</strong> an optional honest-coverage declaration on the human-read ledger (<code>full_text</code> / <code>sections</code> / <code>abstract_only</code> / <code>toc_only</code>) that makes the finalizer's citation promotion read-scope-aware. <strong>Launcher watchdog fix (#545):</strong> removes a pipe-stall that blocked every healthy PreToolUse write-scope-guard call for the full wall-clock bound. Suite → v3.19.0; the three underlying skill versions are unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.18.0 (2026-07-18) — Self-improvement survey integration: advisory quality layers, risk-stratified claim gate, cross-model reviewer & judge tracks</h3><a id="user-content-v3180-2026-07-18--self-improvement-survey-integration-advisory-quality-layers-risk-stratified-claim-gate-cross-model-reviewer--judge-tracks" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.18.0 (2026-07-18) — Self-improvement survey integration: advisory quality layers, risk-stratified claim gate, cross-model reviewer & judge tracks" href="#v3180-2026-07-18--self-improvement-survey-integration-advisory-quality-layers-risk-stratified-claim-gate-cross-model-reviewer--judge-tracks"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Added:</strong> eight quality mechanisms motivated by Ren et al. (2026, arXiv:2607.13104, <em>Self-Improvements in Modern Agentic Systems: A Survey</em>): per-sub-question scope bindings + a Phase E scope-conformance advisory (#547) and search-bounded novelty claims + an E5 novelty classification (#548) — both advisory-only, displayed per-row at the MANDATORY integrity checkpoints; risk-stratified Stage 2.5 claim verification (100% of HIGH-IMPACT claims + a random sentinel, extending the #518 reference tiers to claim level, #549); cache-through wired into the citation-verification gate with an age-based staleness advisory + opt-in live re-validation (#541, closing the v3.11 Delta-2 forward-decl); a consent-gated cross-model reviewer track — one seat of the fixed five-seat panel on the second model family (#540) — and re-review judge independence with a transparent Judge Record (#539); a metamorphic routing/gate robustness eval seed set (#550), which also shipped the reviewer skill's missing zh-TW trigger aliases; and the survey itself as a third human-in-the-loop literature anchor (#542). Independently of the survey track, plugin installs also gain the #544 SessionStart update-available reminder (<code>/plugin update</code> announce when behind; <code>ARS_UPDATE_CHECK=0</code> kill switch). <code>academic-pipeline</code> tracks the suite at v3.18.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.17.0 (2026-07-16) — Pipeline boundary semantics, canonical cross-model handoff envelope, executable panel checker</h3><a id="user-content-v3170-2026-07-16--pipeline-boundary-semantics-canonical-cross-model-handoff-envelope-executable-panel-checker" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.17.0 (2026-07-16) — Pipeline boundary semantics, canonical cross-model handoff envelope, executable panel checker" href="#v3170-2026-07-16--pipeline-boundary-semantics-canonical-cross-model-handoff-envelope-executable-panel-checker"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Fixed:</strong> two under-specified pipeline boundaries closed (#528) — Stage 5's "before finalization: always MANDATORY" now names exactly one checkpoint (the entry gate between Stage 4.5 PASS and Stage 5 dispatch), and Stage 6 gains a defined terminal-acknowledgement vocabulary (<code>finish</code>/<code>end</code>/<code>done</code>/<code>confirm</code>) plus an explicit decline path; all five pipeline surfaces now carry whole-file sha256 content locks (#529) so any further prompt-surface drift fails CI until the hash is updated in the same commit. Blind-checkpoint transport moved to the dispatching layer (#523) — the Bucket A checkpoint owners were being told to execute cross-model transport themselves, which is unexecutable under the runtime Bash deny; the dispatching layer now owns the transport call. <strong>Added:</strong> a canonical <code>[CROSS-MODEL-HANDOFF v1]</code> envelope + normative Python grammar (#527) replaces prose-only enforcement of the owner→dispatcher→owner transport path, pinning agreement/divergence/malformed-result routing across all three checkpoint owners. A defrift lock for the #514 tools allowlist (#524, 74 mutation tests) closes the drift path where a symmetric edit to an agent + its mirror could silently re-add Bash. An executable sprint-contract panel checker (#510) recomputes both v3.6.2 decision layers from the primary artifacts and catches a transcription error in the majority-vote formula. A machine-readable degradation registry (#511 Part A) indexes every graceful-degradation mechanism in the suite, plus a hermetic transport-fixture integration test for the citation-verification gate (#511 Part B) exercising all four resolver clients end-to-end against checked-in synthetic API bodies. <code>academic-pipeline</code> tracks the suite at v3.17.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.16.0 (2026-07-12) — Model tiering, cross-model gate hardening, WP advisory sharpening</h3><a id="user-content-v3160-2026-07-12--model-tiering-cross-model-gate-hardening-wp-advisory-sharpening" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.16.0 (2026-07-12) — Model tiering, cross-model gate hardening, WP advisory sharpening" href="#v3160-2026-07-12--model-tiering-cross-model-gate-hardening-wp-advisory-sharpening"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Added:</strong> opt-in model tiering (#517) — a new <code>ARS_MODEL_TIERING</code> switch with two directions (<code>economy</code> dispatches the 13 execution-type agents one tier below the session model, floor Opus-class; <code>quality-boost</code> steps the judgment-type agents at the integrity gates and final-review surfaces up to the frontier tier); default unset stays byte-equivalent, with the frozen 39-agent classification pinned by a new manifest + lint. Cross-model gate hardening (#518) — risk-stratified verification sampling (HIGH-IMPACT references verified 100% at both gates), blind disagreement checkpoints at the two irreversible decisions (design freeze + final editorial decision), an id-status allowlist for verifier model ids, and a promotion bakeoff protocol; the once-planned generic 6th reviewer is retired, not deferred. GPT-5.6 Sol listed as a provisional cross-model verifier with explicit reasoning-effort control (#515). Korean trigger keywords + routing boundary fixtures by devCharlotte (#452/#509). A CARS introduction-rhetoric + title-crafting reference for the paper writer (#500). <strong>Changed:</strong> the WP research-question advisory generalizes beyond its 20-shell table via the noun-swap test (#501) and a sharpened exemption clause that catches decorated title-form shells (#505) — held-out miss rate 0.34–0.38 → 0.094 with false-fire 0/16 preserved; reviewer calibration protocol now documents the LLM-as-judge leniency direction (FARS anchor, #484); OpenAlex API-key auth + budget-aware 429 handling + arXiv ToU-aligned backoff (#495/#496). <strong>Docs:</strong> THIRD_PARTY.md community directory (#497/#498). <code>academic-pipeline</code> tracks the suite at v3.16.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.15.0 (2026-07-04) — Release-gate hardening, prompt-debt retirement round 2, defrift locks</h3><a id="user-content-v3150-2026-07-04--release-gate-hardening-prompt-debt-retirement-round-2-defrift-locks" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.15.0 (2026-07-04) — Release-gate hardening, prompt-debt retirement round 2, defrift locks" href="#v3150-2026-07-04--release-gate-hardening-prompt-debt-retirement-round-2-defrift-locks"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">A release-discipline-and-hygiene release; no skill-behavior changes. <strong>Added:</strong> three CI gates — the CHANGELOG-covers-merges pre-tag gate (#483), version-consistency invariants 9-11 plus a tag-time re-run gate (#487), and a command-invariants gate pinning the SessionStart announce list to the actual 16-command inventory (#486) — plus two defrift locks: the Phase Boundary enforcement sentence is pinned verbatim across all 23 Bucket A agent blocks, and the SETUP cross-model examples are pinned to each other and to the canonical model tables (#491 → #492). <strong>Changed:</strong> prompt-debt retirement round 2 deep-scans the 17 agents the first pass deferred (#489 → #490): two live self-contradictions fixed in both socratic_mentor agents (stale 15-round quit rules vs the documented typical 20-30-round run), the repo-wide stale enforcement-status sentence corrected at 29 surfaces, few-shot and duplicated-process scaffolds trimmed across 7 agents — verified by a 4-batch parallel audit + independent codex cross-model challenge; audit report under <code>audits/</code>. <strong>Fixed:</strong> DOI badge served from shields.io (#482). <code>academic-pipeline</code> tracks the suite at v3.15.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.14.0 (2026-07-02) — Claude Science importability, eval-comment rendering, prompt-debt retirement</h3><a id="user-content-v3140-2026-07-02--claude-science-importability-eval-comment-rendering-prompt-debt-retirement" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.14.0 (2026-07-02) — Claude Science importability, eval-comment rendering, prompt-debt retirement" href="#v3140-2026-07-02--claude-science-importability-eval-comment-rendering-prompt-debt-retirement"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">A portability-and-polish release; no skill-behavior changes. <strong>Added:</strong> Claude Science importability — the marketplace manifest declares explicit skill paths, so GitHub-API importers that cannot traverse the symlinked <code>skills/</code> directory (Claude Science "Import from GitHub", Windows checkouts) now find all four skills; verified end-to-end on Claude Science, with an import guide in README + SETUP (#480). Eval-harness PR comments render as a one-line verdict + per-task table with the raw JSON folded into <code><details></code>, replacing the raw report dump — display layer only, gate logic byte-identical (#479). <strong>Changed:</strong> expired writing-harness scaffolds retired from four writer-surface agents after the 2026-07 harness-retirement audit (#476/#477 → #478, net −111 prompt lines); a remind-don't-block Platform Port Reminder surfaces the platform-ports policy when a PR adds a new top-level directory (#473). <strong>Docs:</strong> native-reviewed Korean README by devCharlotte (#469/#471); GitHub Copilot repository instructions (#465); auto permission mode recommended over Skip Permissions (#464). The accumulated <code>[Unreleased]</code> backlog (16 entries whose code shipped before the v3.13.0 tag — diff/patch revision mode #390, submission-package verifier #394, eval gold sets #215/#216, and more) is rolled into the versioned record; see <code>CHANGELOG.md</code>. <code>academic-pipeline</code> tracks the suite at v3.14.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.13.0 (2026-06-18) — Hook portability, provider-agnostic verification, guard correctness</h3><a id="user-content-v3130-2026-06-18--hook-portability-provider-agnostic-verification-guard-correctness" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.13.0 (2026-06-18) — Hook portability, provider-agnostic verification, guard correctness" href="#v3130-2026-06-18--hook-portability-provider-agnostic-verification-guard-correctness"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">A minor release hardening the install/runtime surface and extending cross-model reach. <strong>Fixes:</strong> the write-scope guard no longer false-denies a user's own <code>CLAUDE.md</code> under the git-clone + symlink install layout (#459, closing the residual half of #448/#449 — <code>CLAUDE.md</code> is documentation, not a load-bearing enforcement file, so it leaves the infra-protected list while every load-bearing file stays protected); Windows Python hook portability + graceful no-Python degradation via a cross-platform <code>hooks/run_guard.sh</code> launcher that rejects the 0-byte Microsoft Store <code>python3</code> stub and never spams the hook log (#454); <code>draft_writer</code> dual-phase static union documented + POSIX-safe Windows path matching (#451). <strong>Added:</strong> provider-agnostic cross-model verification accepting OpenAI-compatible endpoints (MiMo, DeepSeek, self-hosted) alongside grounded first-party OpenAI, which is never silently downgraded (#455); an opt-in Socratic adjacent-framing probe (STORM-borrowed perspective expansion, <code>ARS_SOCRATIC_ADJACENT_PROBE=1</code>, default OFF, prose-layer only — <code>deep-research</code> 2.10.0 → 2.11.0) (#461). <code>academic-pipeline</code> tracks the suite at v3.13.0; <code>academic-paper</code> and <code>academic-paper-reviewer</code> are unchanged. See <code>CHANGELOG.md</code> for the per-issue detail.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.12.1 (2026-06-15) — Reviewer-response triage modes (PR #433 integration)</h3><a id="user-content-v3121-2026-06-15--reviewer-response-triage-modes-pr-433-integration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.12.1 (2026-06-15) — Reviewer-response triage modes (PR #433 integration)" href="#v3121-2026-06-15--reviewer-response-triage-modes-pr-433-integration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">A patch release folding the genuinely-novel parts of an external contribution into existing skills as modes, per ARS's mode-based architecture. <strong>New modes:</strong> <code>deep-research</code> <code>three-way-scan</code> — a lightweight WHY/HOW/WHAT paper-comparison triage between <code>quick</code> and <code>lit-review</code>, with per-paper shortlists + a cross-paper synthesis (<code>deep-research</code> 2.9.4 → 2.10.0); <code>academic-paper</code> <code>rebuttal-audit</code> — standalone advisory QA of an author's existing rebuttal/response draft against the reviewer comments (per-comment coverage table + gap list + tone/evidence/misread risk flags), which generates nothing and explicitly suppresses Schema 11 / Material Passport writes / <code>ready_to_submit</code> when run standalone (enforced by a <code>check_rebuttal_audit_guard()</code> lint with mutation coverage); plus a <code>revision-coach</code> scope extension to pushback/disagreement posture and non-journal scopes, and <code>/ars-3w</code> + <code>/ars-rebuttal-audit</code> slash commands. Routed by input shape: reviewer comments AND a draft → <code>rebuttal-audit</code>; comments only → <code>revision-coach</code>. Integrated from <a href="https://github.com/Yaobin29">@Yaobin29</a>'s <a href="https://github.com/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/pull/433" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/pull/433/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">PR #433</a>. Suite mode count 25 → 27 (still 4 skills). See <code>CHANGELOG.md</code> for the per-issue detail.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.12.0 (2026-06-08) — Kong auto-research feature track: experiment provenance, figure fidelity, cross-paper contradiction, partial-evidence decomposition</h3><a id="user-content-v3120-2026-06-08--kong-auto-research-feature-track-experiment-provenance-figure-fidelity-cross-paper-contradiction-partial-evidence-decomposition" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.12.0 (2026-06-08) — Kong auto-research feature track: experiment provenance, figure fidelity, cross-paper contradiction, partial-evidence decomposition" href="#v3120-2026-06-08--kong-auto-research-feature-track-experiment-provenance-figure-fidelity-cross-paper-contradiction-partial-evidence-decomposition"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">A minor release shipping the Kong et al. (2026, arXiv:2605.18661) auto-research feature track plus the partial-evidence-trap decomposition work, each reviewed and merged independently. <strong>New features:</strong> Experiment Provenance Intake + claim→experiment alignment — a schema-first evidence-ledger layer for experiment-backed claims, intake-and-alignment only (the scholar runs experiments externally; ARS never executes them) (#260); a Figure/Table Fidelity Gate that checks whether a caption's interpretation follows from the data and whether the manuscript cites the artifact for a claim it supports (#261); a structured Cross-Paper Contradiction inventory making assessed paper-pairs enumerable for scholar confirmation (#262); and sub-claim decomposition before judgment in both the citation judge (#213) and the editorial synthesizer (#214), closing the §F.3.2 partial-evidence trap on both layers. <strong>Guidance + interpretive layer:</strong> concise-output + pressure-stable boundary reinforcement across the report-producing reviewers (#274); a same-family / rubric-aware calibration epistemic note (#273); the retrieved-content instruction/data boundary stated as a standing principle (#367). <strong>Negative scope:</strong> the Kong META (#255) closed with a "Rejected mechanisms" section in <code>POSITIONING.md</code> enumerating the five autonomous mechanisms ARS does not do, plus two Tier D design-lesson docs. <strong>Release-discipline lint:</strong> version-consistency invariants 5–7 (#357) and ARCHITECTURE component-version policing (#345). Plus correctness fixes across the cross-model grounding guards (#346 / #349 / #351), the citation-gate cache key and rationale bounding (#359 / #360 / #361), the eval gold set (#250), and ACL/EMNLP disclosure regrounding (#242). The new schemas, manifest field, and all invariants are additive and backward-compatible. <code>academic-pipeline</code> tracks the suite at v3.12.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged. See <code>CHANGELOG.md</code> for the per-issue detail.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.11.1 (2026-06-06) — Post-ship correctness, hardening & provenance rollup</h3><a id="user-content-v3111-2026-06-06--post-ship-correctness-hardening--provenance-rollup" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.11.1 (2026-06-06) — Post-ship correctness, hardening & provenance rollup" href="#v3111-2026-06-06--post-ship-correctness-hardening--provenance-rollup"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">A patch release rolling up the post-ship fixes surfaced after v3.11.0, each reviewed and merged independently: a cross-model consent-gate extension to the integrity-verification + collaboration-depth paths (#322), a per-entry OpenAlex + Crossref backfill parallelization (#138), and seven correctness/hardening fixes across the citation-existence gate, the v3.10 policy layer, the eval harness, the domain evidence profiles, and the #310 security-boundary edge cases (#323 / #327 / #328 / #329 / #331 / #332 / #333) — including two P1 fixes (#327 domain-profile activation on the no-handoff path, #328 the eval-harness per-class threshold gate). No new features and no breaking schema changes. See <code>CHANGELOG.md</code> for the per-issue detail.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.11.0 (2026-06-04) — Deterministic citation verification gate (#182)</h3><a id="user-content-v3110-2026-06-04--deterministic-citation-verification-gate-182" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.11.0 (2026-06-04) — Deterministic citation verification gate (#182)" href="#v3110-2026-06-04--deterministic-citation-verification-gate-182"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Adds a <strong>deterministic citation-existence verification gate</strong> that runs independently of LLM peer review. Every cited reference is cross-checked against up to four bibliographic indexes — Semantic Scholar + OpenAlex + Crossref + the new <strong>arXiv resolver</strong> (<code>scripts/arxiv_client.py</code>, no API key needed) — and a per-citation <code>lookup_verified</code> status (<code>{true, false, unresolvable}</code>) is written to a unified summary, so a fabricated citation with a provably-bogus DOI/arXiv ID is caught by lookup rather than by hoping a reviewer agent notices. The gate <strong>inherits the v3.10 <code>terminal_policies</code> opt-in model</strong>: detection always runs, but a <code>lookup_verified == false</code> row is terminal <strong>only</strong> when a user opts into <code>terminal_policies.citation_existence == strict</code> — default behavior is advisory and <code>/ars-mark-read</code>-acknowledgeable. <code>false</code> is narrowed to <strong>ID-keyed unmatched</strong> (an exact DOI/arXiv lookup that provably fails), so legitimately-unindexed humanities / non-English / regional citations stay <code>unresolvable</code> and never block (a documented precision-over-recall tradeoff). Ships a persistent SQLite verification cache (<code>~/.cache/ars/verification.db</code>, 90-day TTL) with an <code>/ars-cache-invalidate</code> command, a standalone <code>verification_gate</code> API + <code>verify_passport.py</code> CLI, and a four-index extension (k=0..4) of the v3.9.0 contamination triangulation matrix (all advisory). <code>academic-pipeline</code> tracks the suite at v3.11.0; the other three skill versions are unchanged. Spec: <code>docs/design/2026-05-21-v3.10-182-promote-citation-gate-spec.md</code> (§0 amendment + C-V6).</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.10.0 (2026-06-01) — Triangulation policy layer, Kong survey adoptions, eval harness, scoped-write guard</h3><a id="user-content-v3100-2026-06-01--triangulation-policy-layer-kong-survey-adoptions-eval-harness-scoped-write-guard" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.10.0 (2026-06-01) — Triangulation policy layer, Kong survey adoptions, eval harness, scoped-write guard" href="#v3100-2026-06-01--triangulation-policy-layer-kong-survey-adoptions-eval-harness-scoped-write-guard"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Minor release bundling: the opt-in contamination-triangulation <strong>terminal policy layer</strong> (#127 — default citation behavior byte-equivalent to v3.9.0); <strong>Kong et al. 2026 survey adoptions</strong> — the Rebuttal Commitment Ledger (#256/#266/#268/#269) and discipline-relative domain evidence profiles (#259); the <strong>v3.10 measurement infrastructure</strong> — a generalized eval gold set + ranking-lift CI gate (#184); the <strong>scoped-write guard MVP</strong> (#134) — a deterministic <code>PreToolUse</code> hook that fences the 23 single-phase agents to their own phase directory and denies them Bash (they use the Grep/Glob and structured editing tools instead); the <code>/ars-mark-read</code> plugin commands (#190) plus a broken-on-arrival fix (#195); a Simplified-Chinese README (#185); and CI hardening (#156/#155). <code>academic-paper</code> → v3.2.0 and <code>academic-paper-reviewer</code> → v1.10.0 for the Commitment-Ledger and domain-profile additions; <code>academic-pipeline</code> tracks the suite at v3.10.0. Default skill behavior is unchanged unless a strict policy mode is opted into; the one default-on change is the #134 guard, which constrains the fenced subagents, not user-facing outputs.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.9.4.2 (2026-05-19) — post-ship hotfix for PR #149 CI discipline gates (codex post-ship)</h3><a id="user-content-v3942-2026-05-19--post-ship-hotfix-for-pr-149-ci-discipline-gates-codex-post-ship" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.9.4.2 (2026-05-19) — post-ship hotfix for PR #149 CI discipline gates (codex post-ship)" href="#v3942-2026-05-19--post-ship-hotfix-for-pr-149-ci-discipline-gates-codex-post-ship"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Codex post-ship review of PR #149 (7 CI discipline gates) surfaced 4 P2 findings; v3.9.4.2 hardens 3 of 4. F1: <code>harness-retirement-monthly.yml</code> adds <code>GH_REPO</code> so scheduled runs have repo context for <code>gh issue create</code>. F2: <code>release-cooldown.yml</code> filters <code>PREV_TAG</code> lookup to <code>v*</code> tags so non-release tags cannot bypass cooldown. F3: <code>release-cooldown.yml</code> also reads annotated tag subject + accepts <code>hot-fix</code> spelling (v3.9.2 was previously a false-negative hotfix). PR #157 follow-up: <code>[skip-cooldown]</code> override now read from both commit message AND annotated tag message (self-bootstrapping fix — this tag's cooldown bypass demonstrates F2+F3 work end-to-end). F4 (test-count-monotonic harden) reverted because it surfaced pre-existing <code>scripts/</code> package issue, tracked as #154 (since fixed by PR #158) + re-attempt #155. Closes #152. Follow-ups: #155, #156.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.9.4.1 (2026-05-19) — post-ship hotfix for v3.9.4 temporal verification (#135 codex post-ship)</h3><a id="user-content-v3941-2026-05-19--post-ship-hotfix-for-v394-temporal-verification-135-codex-post-ship" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.9.4.1 (2026-05-19) — post-ship hotfix for v3.9.4 temporal verification (#135 codex post-ship)" href="#v3941-2026-05-19--post-ship-hotfix-for-v394-temporal-verification-135-codex-post-ship"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Codex post-ship review of v3.9.4 caught 4 real bugs that per-task subagent reviewers missed. Hotfix patches all 4: (1) <code>audit()</code> now wires <code>citation_provenance</code> through to P2 and P4 — when a ref slug has <code>confidence: low</code> or <code>conflict</code>, the verifier emits <code>TEMPORAL-METADATA-MISSING</code> instead of using timeline dates as ground truth (spec §3.4 first-party safety check was broken). (2) <code>_date_to_interval</code> parses all schema-valid date shapes including <code>YYYY-MM</code> (Crossref month precision) and <code>YYYY-MM-DD..YYYY-MM-DD</code> (interval); v3.9.4 silently <code>ValueError</code>'d on these and skipped the check. (3) P4 now binds direct date captures when ref markers are absent — sentences like "The 2026 policy enabled the 2020 rollout" actually trigger now. (4) <code>citation_provenance.schema.json</code> <code>confidence:high</code> allOf now requires presence (<code>then.required</code>) in addition to non-null, closing the absent-property bypass. 1561 passed (+12 new tests vs v3.9.4 baseline, 0 regression). ARCHITECTURE.md aligned to current state (was stale at v3.8.0).</p>
</blockquote>

<p dir="auto">#102 closure. v3.7.3 shipped single-index (Semantic Scholar) contamination detection; v3.9.0 extends to three-index triangulation (S2 + OpenAlex + Crossref) as <strong>advisory evidence only</strong>. <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.8.2 (2026-05-17) — #118 uncited audit_tool_failure surface</h3><a id="user-content-v382-2026-05-17--118-uncited-audit_tool_failure-surface" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.8.2 (2026-05-17) — #118 uncited audit_tool_failure surface" href="#v382-2026-05-17--118-uncited-audit_tool_failure-surface"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">#118 closure. The <code>ARS_CLAIM_AUDIT=1</code> uncited constraint-judging path used to silently substitute <code>{"judgment": "NOT_VIOLATED"}</code> on <code>JudgeInvocationError</code>, suppressing HIGH-WARN constraint checks on transient judge outage. v3.8.2 routes those failures through a dedicated <code>uncited_audit_failures[]</code> aggregate at MED-WARN advisory tier, mirroring the cited path INV-14 row but using a dedicated schema because <code>claim_audit_result.ref_slug</code> is required and the uncited path has no ref to bind. The four option-1..4 trade-offs from the #118 issue body landed on option 2 (new aggregate) — option 4 (re-raise and abort) was rejected for the audit-coverage hit on flaky judge endpoints.</p>
</blockquote>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>New <code>uncited_audit_failure.schema.json</code> aggregate</strong> (spec §3.6). One entry per uncited sentence × manifest pair where the constraint judge raised <code>JudgeInvocationError</code>. Same fault-class enum as cited-path INV-14 (<code>judge_timeout</code> / <code>judge_api_error</code> / <code>judge_parse_error</code> / <code>cache_corruption</code> / <code>retrieval_api_error</code> / <code>retrieval_timeout</code> / <code>retrieval_network_error</code>). <code>rule_version: D4-c-v1-uaf-v1</code>.</li>
<li><strong>UAF-INV-1..UAF-INV-6 lint</strong> (spec §6 rule 4d). <code>finding_id</code> uniqueness, scoped_manifest_id cross-array integrity, (M, C) pair integrity when manifest_claim_id non-null, per-(sentence, manifest) dedup, rationale fault_class prefix, cross-aggregate exclusivity vs <code>constraint_violations[]</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Finalizer §5 MED-WARN advisory row</strong>: annotation <code>[CLAIM-AUDIT-TOOL-FAILURE-UNCITED — <fault-class>]</code>, gate passes (retry-next-pass remediation). Formatter REFUSE list unchanged — UAF is advisory.</li>
<li><strong>Pipeline integration</strong> (<code>scripts/claim_audit_pipeline.py</code>): swallow site at line 1211-1224 removed; <code>JudgeInvocationError</code> now emits a UAF row + <code>continue</code>s to the next (sentence, manifest) pair. No fake NOT_VIOLATED reaches <code>constraint_violations[]</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Tests</strong>: 18 new (15 schema/lint TSUAFUncitedAuditFailureInvariants + 3 pipeline integration TP23UncitedJudgeOutageEmitsUAF). Baseline 694 → 712 tests, 0 regression.</li>
<li><strong>Agent doc</strong> (<code>academic-pipeline/agents/claim_ref_alignment_audit_agent.md</code>): Output emission table grows seventh row; Error handling table grows from 3 surfaces to 4 surfaces with the uncited-path UAF row.</li>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.8.0 (2026-05-16) — L3 Claim-Faithfulness Locator + Audit (paired milestone)</h3><a id="user-content-v380-2026-05-16--l3-claim-faithfulness-locator--audit-paired-milestone" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.8.0 (2026-05-16) — L3 Claim-Faithfulness Locator + Audit (paired milestone)" href="#v380-2026-05-16--l3-claim-faithfulness-locator--audit-paired-milestone"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">v3.7.3 + v3.8 close the L3 (claim-faithfulness) gap end-to-end. v3.7.3 ships the locator infrastructure — every citation carries a three-layer anchor so future audits can fetch the cited passage. v3.8 ships the audit pass that consumes those anchors, judges whether the cited source supports the claim, and gate-refuses HIGH-WARN violations at the formatter terminal hard gate. The release also bundles 5 audit-trail-shipped feature PRs accumulated since v3.7.0 (#104 / #105 / #108 / #111 / #115).</p>
</blockquote>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>#103 — <code>claim_ref_alignment_audit_agent</code></strong> (v3.8 PR #121). Opt-in (<code>ARS_CLAIM_AUDIT=1</code>, default OFF) Stage 4→5 audit agent. Judges every sampled citation against retrieved excerpt; emits <code>claim_audit_results[]</code> + <code>claim_intent_manifests[]</code> + <code>claim_drifts[]</code> + <code>uncited_assertions[]</code> + <code>constraint_violations[]</code> aggregates. 8-row finalizer matrix routes HIGH-WARN classes (CLAIM-NOT-SUPPORTED / NEGATIVE-CONSTRAINT-VIOLATION / FABRICATED-REFERENCE / ANCHORLESS / CONSTRAINT-VIOLATION-UNCITED) through the formatter REFUSE rules 6-10. Calibration runner ships with 20-tuple gold set (T-C1 FNR<0.15 + FPR<0.10, T-C2 per-class, T-C3 shape integrity). 8 rounds of dual-track review (R1 codex + Gemini-3.1-pro-preview, R2-R8 codex-only after Gemini quota exhausted); trajectory R1 4P1+2P2 → R8 0P1+4P2 ship gate.</li>
<li><strong>v3.7.3 — Three-Layer Citation Emission + contamination signals</strong> (PR #98). <code>synthesis_agent</code> / <code>draft_writer_agent</code> / <code>report_compiler_agent</code> gain <code>## Three-Layer Citation Emission (v3.7.3)</code> H2. Every <code><!--ref:slug--></code> carries <code><!--anchor:<kind>:<value>--></code> with <code><kind> ∈ {quote, page, section, paragraph, none}</code> (quote anchors capped at 25 words, URL-encoded). <code>pipeline_orchestrator_agent</code> finalizer becomes 5-cell with precedence-zero NO-LOCATOR check. <code>formatter_agent</code> adds explicit hard-gate refusal for <code>[UNVERIFIED CITATION — NO QUOTE OR PAGE LOCATOR]</code>. <code>literature_corpus_entry.schema.json</code> adds optional <code>contamination_signals: { preprint_post_llm_inflection, semantic_scholar_unmatched }</code> object. <code>bibliography_agent</code> computes both signals at ingest. 11-round review trajectory (Codex×10 + Gemini cross-model×1) closed 22 findings. Spec: <code>docs/design/2026-05-12-ars-v3.7.3-claim-faithfulness-and-contaminated-source-spec.md</code>. External motivation: Zhao et al. arXiv:2605.07723 (2026-05).</li>
<li><strong>#108 — AI disclosure policy-anchor renderer</strong> (audit-trail-shipped 2026-05-14). Adds PRISMA-trAIce / ICMJE / Nature / IEEE policy-anchor disclosure paths alongside the existing venue-track renderer.</li>
<li><strong>#111 — <code>slr_lineage</code> emission on systematic-review → academic-paper handoff</strong> (2026-05-15). Schema 9 optional boolean <code>slr_lineage</code> field; producer <code>pipeline_orchestrator_agent</code> writes at every handoff transition; consumer <code>disclosure</code> mode dispatches <code>--policy-anchor=prisma-trAIce</code> per the §4.3 G2 invariant track gate.</li>
<li><strong>#104 — README motivation: Zhao et al. corpus-scale evidence anchor</strong> (2026-05-15). README + <code>README.zh-TW.md</code> motivation section frames the v3.7.x line against Zhao et al.'s 146,932 hallucinated-citation finding.</li>
<li><strong>#105 — v3.7.3 contamination_signals backfill migration tool</strong> (2026-05-15). <code>scripts/migrate_literature_corpus_to_v3_7_3.py</code> retro-computes both contamination signals across pre-v3.7.3 passports.</li>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.7.0 (2026-05-05) — Claude Code Plugin Packaging</h3><a id="user-content-v370-2026-05-05--claude-code-plugin-packaging" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.7.0 (2026-05-05) — Claude Code Plugin Packaging" href="#v370-2026-05-05--claude-code-plugin-packaging"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Plugin packaging upgrade: ARS now installs in one line on Claude Code CLI / VS Code / JetBrains via <code>/plugin marketplace add Imbad0202/academic-research-skills</code> + <code>/plugin install academic-research-skills</code>. The traditional <code>git clone + symlink to ~/.claude/skills/</code> flow continues to work — both tracks are first-class.</p>
</blockquote>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Plugin manifest + marketplace metadata</strong> (Phase 1, PR #68). <code>.claude-plugin/plugin.json</code> declares the suite (4 skills auto-discovered from <code>skills/</code> directory via relative symlinks). <code>.claude-plugin/marketplace.json</code> registers the plugin so a single GitHub-hosted endpoint serves both the marketplace listing and the plugin source. README + <code>README.zh-TW.md</code> + <code>docs/SETUP.md</code> carry dual-track install instructions.</li>
<li><strong>10 slash commands</strong> at <code>commands/ars-*.md</code> (Phase 2.1, PR #69) mapping <code>MODE_REGISTRY.md</code> entries to <code>/ars-<mode></code> triggers. Model routing is pinned in each command's frontmatter — <code>opus</code> for <code>full</code> and <code>revision-coach</code> (architectural / review-interpretation depth), <code>sonnet</code> for the other 8. No Haiku per project policy.</li>
<li><strong>3 plugin-shipped agents</strong> at <code>agents/*_agent.md</code> (Phase 2.1, PR #69) as relative symlinks to the v3.6.7-hardened downstream agents in <code>deep-research/agents/</code>: <code>synthesis_agent</code>, <code>research_architect_agent</code>, <code>report_compiler_agent</code>. Underscore filenames preserved to keep <code>scripts/check_v3_6_7_pattern_protection.py</code> hard-pinned paths and INV-3 manifest-confined Clause 1 invariant intact. Symlinks (not copies) preserve a single source of truth and prevent the Pattern C3 attack surface that v3.6.7 §6 inversion sweep + INV-1/2/3 lint closes. (Materialized to real byte-identical copies in #413 — relative symlinks break Windows checkouts without <code>core.symlinks</code> and zip-download installs; the single-source guarantee moved to the <code>scripts/check_agents_mirror_sync.py</code> byte-equality CI lint.)</li>
<li><strong><code>model: inherit</code></strong> added to those three source agent frontmatters. Inherit chosen over pinning <code>sonnet</code> so an opus session running ARS full pipeline keeps opus agents (instead of being capped). The user's <code>~/.claude/hooks/warn-agent-no-model.sh</code> PreToolUse hook gates Haiku at the dispatching boundary, so <code>inherit</code> resolves through an already-Haiku-free model.</li>
<li><strong>SessionStart announce hook</strong> at <code>hooks/hooks.json</code> + <code>scripts/announce-ars-loaded.sh</code> (Phase 2.2, PR #70). When the plugin loads, the hook injects an <code>additionalContext</code> listing the 10 slash commands, the 3 plugin agents, and a token-budget pointer into the LLM's first turn. <code>startup</code> and <code>clear</code> source values get the full announce; <code>resume</code> and <code>compact</code> get a one-line ack to avoid burning context. Bash 3.2 compatible — runs on macOS stock <code>/bin/bash</code> with no <code>brew install bash</code> requirement.</li>
<li><strong>Phase 2.2 scope reduction</strong>: a <code>SubagentStop → run_codex_audit.sh</code> codex audit hook was scoped out for v3.7.0 due to a contract gap (the SubagentStop payload carries no stage/deliverable info, so the wrapper would have to half-infer required arguments) and an invoker-class boundary (<code>run_codex_audit.sh</code> lines 4–7 forbid same-session in-LLM invocation; PostToolUse fires inside the producing session). Real audit-hook integration deferred to a future release when ARS gains a stage/deliverable propagation contract. See <code>docs/design/2026-04-30-ars-v3.7.0-plugin-packaging-roadmap.md</code> Update note 2026-05-05 (Phase 2.2 scope reduction).</li>
<li><strong><code>docs/PERFORMANCE.md</code> + <code>.zh-TW.md</code></strong> gain a "v3.7.0 Plugin agents and model routing" subsection explaining the inherit semantics and current 3-agent scope boundary.</li>
<li><strong>Codex review chain across the three PRs</strong>: 8 inline iterative rounds + 3 fresh PR-level rounds, all converging to 0 P0/P1/P2 findings before merge. The Phase 2.2 fresh PR review caught one P2 (unquoted <code>${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}</code> breaking install paths with spaces) that the inline rounds missed — confirms the value of separating implementation review (inline) from contract review (fresh).</li>
<li><strong>What did NOT change</strong>: the four skill directories, all 25 modes, agent prompts, schema files, and lint contracts. Plugin packaging only adds new top-level surface (<code>commands/</code>, <code>agents/</code>, <code>hooks/</code>, <code>.claude-plugin/</code>, <code>skills/</code> symlink dir, three plugin-agent <code>model: inherit</code> frontmatter additions). Existing 4.3k clone-install users see no breaking change.</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.6.3 (2026-04-23) — Opt-in Passport Reset Boundary</h3><a id="user-content-v363-2026-04-23--opt-in-passport-reset-boundary" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.6.3 (2026-04-23) — Opt-in Passport Reset Boundary" href="#v363-2026-04-23--opt-in-passport-reset-boundary"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Opt-in passport reset boundary</strong> (<code>ARS_PASSPORT_RESET=1</code>). Promotes every FULL checkpoint to a context-reset boundary. New <code>resume_from_passport=<hash></code> mode lets users resume in a fresh Claude Code session from the Material Passport ledger alone. <code>systematic-review</code> mode with the flag ON makes reset mandatory at every FULL checkpoint; other modes treat reset as the flag-gated default. Flag OFF preserves pre-v3.6.3 behavior byte-for-byte.</li>
<li>Schema 9 gains an append-only <code>reset_boundary[]</code> ledger with two entry kinds (<code>kind: boundary</code> + <code>kind: resume</code>). Hash uses JSON Canonical Form + SHA-256 with canonical placeholder for self-reference safety. Optional <code>pending_decision</code> handles MANDATORY branch choices.</li>
<li>New <code>scripts/check_passport_reset_contract.py</code> CI lint: every mention of the flag must co-locate a pointer to the authoritative protocol doc.</li>
<li>Protocol doc: <code>academic-pipeline/references/passport_as_reset_boundary.md</code>.</li>
<li><code>docs/PERFORMANCE.md</code> updated with long-running-session guidance.</li>
<li>No breaking changes. Flag default is OFF.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.6.2 (2026-04-23) — Reviewer Sprint Contract Hard Gate</h3><a id="user-content-v362-2026-04-23--reviewer-sprint-contract-hard-gate" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.6.2 (2026-04-23) — Reviewer Sprint Contract Hard Gate" href="#v362-2026-04-23--reviewer-sprint-contract-hard-gate"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">v3.6.2 introduces Schema 13 sprint contracts and a hard-gate orchestration that forces reviewers to pre-commit their scoring plan before reading the paper. Reviewer-only first test case; writer/evaluator deferred to v3.6.4. See CHANGELOG.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Schema 13 sprint contract</strong> with <code>panel_size</code>, <code>acceptance_dimensions</code>, <code>failure_conditions</code> (with <code>severity</code> precedence + panel-relative <code>cross_reviewer_quantifier</code>), <code>measurement_procedure</code>, optional <code>override_ladder</code>, bounded <code>agent_amendments</code>. Validator: <code>scripts/check_sprint_contract.py</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Two-call hard gate.</strong> Reviewers run paper-content-blind Phase 1 + paper-visible Phase 2; Phase 1 output is wrapped in <code><phase1_output>...</phase1_output></code> data delimiter to narrow the self-injection surface.</li>
<li><strong>Synthesizer three-step mechanical protocol.</strong> Build cross-reviewer matrix → evaluate each <code>failure_condition</code> with panel-relative quantifier + recognised expression vocabulary → resolve precedence by <code>severity</code>. Forbidden-ops list explicit in <code>editorial_synthesizer_agent</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Two reviewer templates ship</strong> (<code>shared/contracts/reviewer/full.json</code> panel 5; <code>shared/contracts/reviewer/methodology_focus.json</code> panel 2). <code>reviewer_re_review</code>, <code>reviewer_calibration</code>, <code>reviewer_guided</code> are reserved in the schema enum but ship without contract templates in v3.6.2; they retain pre-v3.6.2 behaviour. <code>reviewer_quick</code> is excluded from the enum entirely.</li>
<li><code>academic-paper-reviewer</code> SKILL version: <code>1.8.1 → 1.9.0</code>. <code>academic-pipeline</code> SKILL version: <code>3.5.1 → 3.6.2</code> (suite-version invariant). Suite version bumped to <code>3.6.2</code>.</li>
<li>See spec <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/docs/design/2026-04-23-ars-v3.6.2-sprint-contract-design.md"><code>docs/design/2026-04-23-ars-v3.6.2-sprint-contract-design.md</code></a> and protocol <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/academic-paper-reviewer/references/sprint_contract_protocol.md"><code>academic-paper-reviewer/references/sprint_contract_protocol.md</code></a>.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.5.1 (2026-04-22) — Opt-in Socratic Reading-Check Probe</h3><a id="user-content-v351-2026-04-22--opt-in-socratic-reading-check-probe" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.5.1 (2026-04-22) — Opt-in Socratic Reading-Check Probe" href="#v351-2026-04-22--opt-in-socratic-reading-check-probe"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">v3.5.1 adds an opt-in honesty probe to the Socratic Mentor (<code>ARS_SOCRATIC_READING_PROBE=1</code>). Default off. See CHANGELOG.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Opt-in reading-check probe</strong>: when <code>ARS_SOCRATIC_READING_PROBE=1</code> is set, the Socratic Mentor fires a one-time honesty probe during goal-oriented sessions where the user has cited a specific paper. Decline is logged without penalty. Outcome flows into the Research Plan Summary and Stage 6 AI Self-Reflection Report. No new agent, no schema change.</li>
<li><code>deep-research</code> SKILL version: <code>2.9.0 → 2.9.1</code>. <code>academic-pipeline</code> SKILL version: <code>3.5.0 → 3.5.1</code>. Suite version bumped to <code>3.5.1</code>.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.5.0 (2026-04-21) — Collaboration Depth Observer</h3><a id="user-content-v350-2026-04-21--collaboration-depth-observer" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.5.0 (2026-04-21) — Collaboration Depth Observer" href="#v350-2026-04-21--collaboration-depth-observer"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>New agent</strong>: <code>collaboration_depth_agent</code> in <code>academic-pipeline</code> (Agent Team grows from 3 to 4). Invoked at every FULL/SLIM checkpoint and at pipeline completion; scores user-AI collaboration against a 4-dimension rubric. <strong>Advisory only — never blocks progression.</strong> MANDATORY checkpoints (Stages 2.5 / 4.5 integrity gates) do NOT invoke the observer.</li>
<li><strong>New rubric</strong>: <a href="/Imbad0202/academic-research-skills/blob/main/shared/collaboration_depth_rubric.md"><code>shared/collaboration_depth_rubric.md</code></a> v1.0. Dimensions: Delegation Intensity, Cognitive Vigilance, Cognitive Reallocation, Zone Classification (Zone 1 / Zone 2 / Zone 3). Based on Wang, S., & Zhang, H. (2026). "Pedagogical partnerships with generative AI in higher education: how dual cognitive pathways paradoxically enable transformative learning." <em>International Journal of Educational Technology in Higher Education</em>, 23:11. DOI <a href="https://doi.org/10.1186/s41239-026-00585-x" rel="nofollow">10.1186/s41239-026-00585-x</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Cross-model divergence flagged, not averaged</strong>: when <code>ARS_CROSS_MODEL</code> is set the observer runs on both models; dimension disagreement > 2 points is reported rather than silently smoothed. <code>ARS_CROSS_MODEL_SAMPLE_INTERVAL</code> escape hatch for cost trade-off.</li>
<li><strong>Short-stage guard</strong>: stages with fewer than 5 user turns inject a static <code>insufficient_evidence</code> block instead of dispatching the full-model observer.</li>
<li><strong>Anti-sycophancy discipline</strong>: scores ≥ 7 require specific dialogue-turn citations; Zone 3 triggers re-audit; no motivational framing.</li>
<li><code>academic-pipeline</code> SKILL version: <code>3.3.0 → 3.4.0</code>. Suite version bumped to <code>3.5.0</code>. New lint <code>scripts/check_collaboration_depth_rubric.py</code> + 10 tests.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">v3.4.0 (2026-04-20) — Compliance Agent + Schema 12</h3><a id="user-content-v340-2026-04-20--compliance-agent--schema-12" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: v3.4.0 (2026-04-20) — Compliance Agent + Schema 12" href="#v340-2026-04-20--compliance-agent--schema-12"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Compliance Agent</strong> (shared): single mode-aware agent running PRISMA-trAIce 17 items (SR mode only) + RAISE 4 principles + 8-role matrix. Hooks existing Stage 2.5 / 4.5 Integrity Gates; tier-based block (Mandatory → block, HR → warn, R/O → info). Non-SR entries run principles-only, warn-only.</li>
<li><strong>Schema 12 compliance_report</strong> appended to Material Passport via <code>compliance_history[]</code> (append-only).</li>
<li><strong>3-round user-override ladder</strong> auto-injects <code>disclosure_addendum</code> into manuscript. No detection evasion possible.</li>
<li><strong>Calibration with transparent reporting</strong>, no hard FNR/FPR gate — self-consistent with <code>task_type: open-ended</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Upstream freshness CI</strong> warns on PRISMA-trAIce drift (non-blocking).</li>
<li><strong>Long-running session docs</strong>: Material Passport as cross-session resume mechanism.</li>
</ul>
